item.title: 每个开发人员都应该知道的 20 个 Git 命令 | 20 Git Commands Every Developer Should Know
item.description:
克里希纳阿加瓦尔

发表于在这篇文章中，我只想写一个速查表。 它将包括我在第一篇文章中分享的命令，但它也将包括一些新的 git 命令。 最近，对于各种高级项目，我一直在与不同的团队合作。 有一些命令已成为我的主要内容。

那么让我们开始吧：

此命令用于将项目初始化为 git 存储库。

此命令用于添加或连接到远程存储库。

此命令用于查看连接的远程存储库。

此命令用于查看本地存储库中文件的状态。 是否跟踪文件？ 未追踪？ 修改的？

此命令用于暂存已修改或未跟踪的文件。

混帐添加。
此命令用于暂存所有未暂存的文件。

此命令用于取消暂存文件。

此命令用于提交暂存文件。

此命令用于提交暂存文件并为提交历史提供提交消息。

此命令用于将提交的文件推送到指定分支中的远程存储库（又名 GitHub）。 首次将文件推送到远程存储库时使用此命令。 它将确定您将这些文件推送到的位置。 下次推送文件时，可以使用 git push

此命令用于将提交的文件推送到远程存储库。 您只能在使用上一个命令推送文件之后使用此命令将文件推送到远程存储库。

此命令用于获取本地存储库的最新版本。 它检查新文件、新分支、删除等。

此命令用于获取您刚刚获取的信息并将其拉入本地存储库。 这会将您的本地存储库更新为远程存储库的最新版本。

此命令用于从远程存储库 (GitHub) 中删除文件，而不在本地存储库中删除它。

  1. 此命令用于预览本地和远程存储库中的所有分支。

    此命令用于预览本地存储库中的所有分支（也就是您已经访问过的分支）。

    此命令用于在本地存储库中创建新分支。

    该命令用于切换分支。 这特别适用于您第一次访问分支（在 GitHub/远程存储库中创建）时。

    该命令用于切换到您之前访问过的分支。

    该命令用于将两个分支合并在一起。 为此，请输入要继承更改的分支。 您将与此命令一起使用的分支名称是将提供更改的分支。

    示例：主分支将继承开发分支的代码

    此命令用于中止合并。

    如果没有冲突错误，合并将始终成功。 因此，此中止只能在合并失败的情况下使用。

    你怎么知道这可以使用？

    对于初学者，您的终端会说合并失败。 它还可能会告诉您修复合并冲突。

    这是另一个标志：

    看看第一行的最后。 在括号中，它说(master) 。 这是因为我们在 master 分支中。 如果你在开发分支，它会说(develop) 。

    如果您的合并失败，它会说(master|merging)或类似的东西。 也许它说合并，或者它是一个正斜杠，或者你在另一个分支中。 无论如何，你明白了。

    这表明您的合并失败。

    该命令用于将两个分支合并在一起。 如果存在合并冲突，此命令将假设您更喜欢在提到的分支中所做的更改（而不是当前分支）。

    此命令将清除您在本地存储库中所做的所有更改，并将其更新为提交到 GitHub 的最新版本。

    此命令用于删除本地存储库中未跟踪的文件

    此命令用于删除本地存储库中未跟踪的目录。 您也可以将其组合到 git clean -fd 中以同时执行这两项操作。

    它有助于追踪代码有效的提交和无效的提交，因此，追踪将错误引入代码的提交。

    此命令将当前分支向后移动两次提交，有效地从项目历史记录中删除了我们刚刚创建的两个快照。 它恢复意外提交并保留更改。

    感谢您的阅读！ 如果您有一些您一直使用的命令，请分享它们！

    如果您觉得这有帮助，请务必通过点赞来表示您的支持，如果您想在他们的项目中帮助他人，我们将不胜感激！ 让我知道你对此的看法！ 快乐学习！

    在GitHub和 DEV 上关注我。

    一旦暂停，krishnaagarwal 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

    一旦解除暂停，krishnaagarwal 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦未发布，krishnaagarwal 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 krishnaagarwal 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。



原文:

Krishna Agarwal

Posted on Jul 21

20 Git Command I Use All The Time — Git CheatSheet

In this article, I just want to lay down a quick cheat sheet. It will include commands that I shared in that first article, but it will also include some new git commands. Recently, for various senior projects, I have been collaborating with different teams. There are a few commands that have become a staple for me.

So let's get started:

1. git init
This command is used to initialize a project as a git repository.

2. git remote add origin Example:

git remote add origin https://github.com/MrKrishnaAgarwal/Git-CheatSheet.git

This command is used to add or connect to a remote repository.

3. git remote
This command is used to view connected remote repositories.

4. git status
This command is used to view the status of files in your local repository. Are files tracked? untracked? modified?

5. git add Example:

git add index.html style.css style.scss

This command is used to stage modified or untracked files.

git add .
This command is used to stage ALL unstaged files.

6. git reset
This command is used to unstage files.

7. git commit
This command is used to commit staged files.

git commit -m “ Example:

git commit -m "added navigation bar"

This command is used to commit staged files AND provide a commit message for the commit history.

8. git push -u origin Example:

git push -u origin master

This command is used to push committed files to the remote repository(aka GitHub) in the specified branch. Use this command for the very first time you push files to the remote repository. It will establish where you are pushing these files to. The next time(s) you push files, you can use git push

git push
This command is used to push committed files to the remote repository. You can only use this command to push files to the remote repository AFTER having pushed files using the previous command.

9. git fetch
This command is used to fetch the most updated version of your local repository. It checks for new files, new branches, deletions etc.

10. git pull
This command is used to take that information you just fetched and pull it into your local repository. This updates your local repository to the most updated version of your remote repository.

11. git rm -r — cached Example:

git rm -r — cached config.js

This command is used to remove a file from the remote repository(GitHub) without deleting it in your local repository.

  1. git branch This command is used to preview the branch you are currently on

git branch -a
This command is used to preview all the branches in your local and remote repository.

git branch -r
This command is used to preview all the branches in your local repository (aka branches you have already visited).

git branch
This command is used to create a new branch in your local repository.

13. git checkout — track origin/ Example:

git checkout --track origin/develop

This command is used to switch branches. This is specifically for when you are visiting a branch (created in GitHub/remote repository) for the very first time.

14. git checkout Example:

git checkout master
git checkout develop

This command is used to switch to branches you have already visited before.

15. git merge
This command is used to merge two branches together. To do this, enter the branch that you want to inherit the changes. And the branch name you would use along with this command is the branch that will provide the changes.

Example: master branch will inherit code from develop branch

16. git merge — abort
This command is used to abort a merge.

If there are no conflict errors, merges will always be successful. Ergo, this abort can only be used in situations where a merge failed.

How will you know this can be used?

For starters, your terminal will say merge failed. It may also tell you to fix the merge conflicts.

Here is another sign:

Look at the very end of the first line. In parentheses, it says (master). This is because we are in the master branch. If you are in the develop branch, it would say (develop).

If your merge fails, it will say (master|merging) or something like that. Maybe it says merge or maybe it’s a forward slash or maybe you’re in another branch. Regardless, you get the idea.

This indicates your merge failed.

git merge --abort would just abort the merge entirely.

17. git merge -X theirs Example:

git merge -X theirs develop

This command is used to merge two branches together. And if there are merging conflicts, this command will just assume that you’d prefer the changes made in the mentioned branch (rather than the current one).

18. git reset — hard HEAD
This command will erase all the changes you’ve made in your local repository and update it to the latest version that was committed to GitHub.

19. git clean -f
This command is used to delete untracked files in your local repository

20. git clean -d
This command is used to delete untracked directories in your local repository. You can also combine it into git clean -fd to do both.

21. git bisect (Added By Shai Almog)

The git bisect command is used to discover the commit that has introduced a bug in the code. It helps track down the commit where the code works and the commit where it does not, hence, tracking down the commit that introduced the bug into the code.

22. git reset HEAD^ (Added By Joe Jordan)

This command moves the current branch backward by two commits, effectively removing the two snapshots we just created from the project history. it reverts an accidental commit and keep the changes.

Thank you for reading! If you have some commands that you use all the time, please share them!

If you found this helpful, make sure to show your support with a like, and if you want to help others in their projects, a share would be greatly appreciated! Let me know what you think about this! Happy Learning!

Follow me on GitHub and DEV.

Once suspended, krishnaagarwal will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, krishnaagarwal will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by krishnaagarwal will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If krishnaagarwal is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

item.title: 两年的 dev.to - 让我们用“Dance Dance Revolution”克隆来庆祝吧！ 💃🕺🎉 💃🕺🎉 | Two years of dev.to - let's celebrate with a "Dance Dance Revolution" clone! 💃🕺🎉
item.description:
帕斯卡尔·托梅尔

发表于是时候庆祝了！

今天是我在 dev.to 上的两周年纪念日！ 总共发布了 52 个帖子，大约每两周发布一个！

还有什么比“Dance Dance Revolution”更好的庆祝方式呢？ 游戏规则解释得很快：

  • 盒子从顶部从四个不同的车道下来
  • 一旦车道进入得分区（甚至更好：绿色甜蜜点！），您需要按正确的箭头键来得分
  • 任何不正确的按键（或太早或太晚）都会让你失去 10 条生命中的一条

但是，一些免责声明：

  • 代码到目前为止还不是最干净的！
  • 音乐是免版税的
  • 有时，箭头图标需要几秒钟才能加载
  • 在开始新游戏之前等待所有箭头消失。 否则它可能会预先扣除生命

此克隆最好在带有键盘的计算机上和单独的窗口中播放。

玩得开心！

你的高分是多少？ 在下方留言！

我希望你喜欢阅读这篇文章，就像我喜欢写这篇文章一样！ 如果是这样，请留下一个❤️或一个🦄 ！ 我在空闲时间写科技文章，喜欢偶尔喝一杯咖啡。

如果你想支持我的努力，你可以给我一杯咖啡☕或在 Twitter 上关注我🐦 ！ 您也可以直接通过Paypal支持我！

一旦被暂停，thormeier 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，thormeier 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发表，thormeier 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 thormeier 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。



原文:

Pascal Thormeier

Posted on Jul 21

Time to celebrate!

Today marks my two-year anniversary on dev.to! With a total of 52 published posts, that's roughly one every two weeks!

And what better way to celebrate than with "Dance Dance Revolution"? The game's rules are explained quickly:

  • Boxes come down four different lanes from the top
  • As soon as the lanes hit a scoring zone (or even better: the green sweet-spot!), you need to press the correct arrow key to score points
  • Any non-correct key-press (or too early or too late) will cost you one of 10 lives

Some disclaimers, though:

  • The code is by far not the cleanest!
  • The music is royalty-free
  • Sometimes, the arrow icons take a few seconds to load
  • Wait for all the arrows to disappear before starting a new game. It might deduct lives upfront otherwise

This clone is best played on a computer with a keyboard and in a separate window.

Have fun!

What's your high score? Leave a comment down below!

I hope you enjoyed reading this article as much as I enjoyed writing it! If so, leave a ❤️ or a 🦄! I write tech articles in my free time and like to drink coffee every once in a while.

If you want to support my efforts, you can offer me a coffee ☕ or follow me on Twitter 🐦! You can also support me directly via Paypal!

Mehedi Hasan Sagor - Jun 26

Mehedi Hasan Sagor - Jun 26

Mehedi Hasan Sagor - Jun 26

Once suspended, thormeier will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, thormeier will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by thormeier will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If thormeier is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.
item.author: thormeier

item.title: 使用 OpenTelemetry 和 SigNoz 进行 Redis 监控 | Redis Monitoring with OpenTelemetry and SigNoz
item.description:
在这篇文章中，我们将向您展示如何使用 SigNoz（一个开源的全栈 APM）设置 Redis 监控。 除了从 Redis 服务器捕获指标外，您还可以使用 OpenTelemetry 捕获日志和跟踪。

什么是 OpenTelemetry？

它遵循规范驱动的开发。 客户端库可在多种编程语言中实现一致的检测体验。 它可以用作数据处理系统来创建一致的数据管道。

使用 OpenTelemetry 的最大优势之一是它为用户提供了选择后端分析工具的自由。 它将专有的 SaaS 代理替换为在整个应用程序架构中一致的开源标准。 它不提供数据存储和可视化层。 这就是SigNoz 发挥作用的地方。 使用 OpenTelemetry 收集器收集 Redis 指标

收集器可以接收所有类型的遥测数据，包括日志、指标和跟踪。

您可以借助 OpenTelemetry Collector 从单个 Redis 实例中收集 Redis INFO 数据。

处理器

处理器执行各种任务，例如数据清理、数据规范化、路由和采样。

  • 出口商

    导出器用于将处理后的遥测数据导出到各种后端。

    • 一旦解除暂停，signoz 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦取消发布，signoz 发布的所有帖子都将被隐藏，并且只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 signoz 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。



    原文:

    In this post, we will show you how to set up Redis monitoring with SigNoz - an open-source full-stack APM. SigNoz captures data using OpenTelemetry, which is becoming the world standard for instrumenting cloud-native applications. Apart from capturing metrics from your Redis server, you can also capture logs and traces with OpenTelemetry.

    What is OpenTelemetry?

    OpenTelemetry is an open-source collection of tools, APIs, and SDKs that aims to standardize how we generate and collect telemetry data. It follows a specification-driven development. The OpenTelemetry specification has design and implementation guidelines for how the instrumentation libraries should be implemented.

    OpenTelemetry is incubated under Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF), the same foundation which incubated Kubernetes. OpenTelemetry solves the challenge of instrumenting polyglot cloud-native applications. The client libraries enable a consistent instrumentation experience in multiple programming languages. OpenTelemetry provides a stand-alone service called the Collector. It can be used as a data processing system to create consistent data pipelines.

    One of the biggest advantages of using OpenTelemetry is that it provides users the freedom to choose a backend analysis tool. It replaces proprietary SaaS agents with an open source standard consistent across the entire application architecture. OpenTelemetry enables engineering teams to reduce dependency issues by allowing access to a single knowledge base for instrumenting applications.

    It does not provide a data storage and visualization layer. And that’s where SigNoz comes into the picture. SigNoz is an open source APM built to support OpenTelemetry natively. Redis metrics are captured with the help of OpenTelemetry Collector.

    Collecting Redis Metrics with OpenTelemetry Collector

    The client libraries of OpenTelemetry are used mainly for traces, logs, and application metrics. The Collector can receive all types of telemetry, including logs, metrics, and traces.

    Redis exposes a number of performance metrics and stats through the INFO command. You can collect the Redis INFO data from a single Redis instance with the help of OpenTelemetry Collector.

    The OpenTelemetry Collector provides a telemetry processing system that can be configured to import and export data in many common formats. The three main components of OpenTelemetry Collector are:

  • Processors

    Processors perform a variety of tasks like data scrubbing, data normalization, routing, and sampling.

  • Exporters

    Exporters are used to export the processed telemetry data to a variety of backends.

    • SigNoz installation ships with two instances of OpenTelemetry Collectors. One Collector is meant for push-based metrics collection, while the other is meant for pull-based metrics collection. For this post, we will be using the pull-based OpenTelemetry Collector.

    The collectors write the data to ClickHouse, the database used by SigNoz. Then, a query service talks to the ClickHouse DB, as shown in the picture, to create visualizations that are served by a frontend web application.

    For collecting and visualizing Redis metrics, let us first install SigNoz.

    Installing SigNoz

    SigNoz is an open-source APM that can be self-hosted within your infrastructure.

    It is easy to get started with SigNoz. It can be installed on macOS or Linux computers in just three steps by using a simple installation script.

    The install script automatically installs Docker Engine on Linux. However, you must manually install Docker Engine on macOS before running the install script.

    git clone -b main https://github.com/SigNoz/signoz.git
cd signoz/deploy/
./install.sh

    You can visit our documentation for instructions on how to install SigNoz using Docker Swarm and Helm Charts.

    Once installed, you can access SigNoz UI at port 3301 -  http://localhost:3301. You will get a sign-up page. If you’re a first-time user, you can create an account using Create an account.

    SigNoz provides role-based access control features. Creating an account for the first time creates an Admin account.

    Once you sign in, you will have access to all the tabs of SigNoz. For monitoring Redis metrics, we will be using the Dashboards tab.

    SigNoz APM dashboard - comes with metrics monitoring, distributed tracing, and exceptions monitoring, among other things.

    Steps to capture Redis Metrics with Otel-Collector

    First of all, ensure that your Redis server and redis-cli are running properly.

    redis-cli info | grep uptime_in_seconds

    If you’re running command from a different host or port, add the following arguments to the command:

    redis-cli -h <host> -p <port> info | grep uptime_in_seconds

    You will see an output as follows:

    Now you need to configure the OpenTelemetry Redis receiver. The collector settings are configured using yaml files.

    Open up the otel-collector-metrics-config.yaml file located at the following address inside the installation folder of SigNoz:

    deploy/docker/clickhouse-setup/otel-collector-metrics-config.yaml

    You need to make two changes to enable OpenTelemetry Collector to receive Redis metrics:

    • Enable the Redis receiver In the config yaml file, you need to add redis as one of the receivers.

      receivers: redis: endpoint: "localhost:6379" collection_interval: 60s password: $REDIS_PASSWORD

    • Configuring the data pipeline

      As mentioned above, OpenTelemetry Collectors act as a telemetry processing system with a configurable data pipeline. We will need to add redis in the data pipeline.


      pipelines: metrics: receivers: [redis, prometheus] processors: [batch] exporters: [clickhousemetricswrite]

      The above pipeline sets the Redis data pipeline in which it is received as Redis metrics and exported to be written in ClickHouse, the database used by SigNoz to store telemetry data.

    You can have a look at the otel-collector-metrics-config.yaml file on our GitHub repo. The final config file along with Redis receiver looks like below:

    receivers: otlp: protocols: grpc: http: prometheus: config: scrape_configs: # otel-collector internal metrics - job_name: "otel-collector" scrape_interval: 60s static_configs: - targets: - otel-collector:8888 # otel-collector-metrics internal metrics - job_name: "otel-collector-metrics" scrape_interval: 60s static_configs: - targets: - localhost:8888 # SigNoz span metrics - job_name: "signozspanmetrics-collector" scrape_interval: 60s static_configs: - targets: - otel-collector:8889 #Redis metrics receiver redis: endpoint: "host.docker.internal:6379" collection_interval: 60s processors: batch: send_batch_size: 10000 send_batch_max_size: 11000 timeout: 10s # memory_limiter: # # 80% of maximum memory up to 2G # limit_mib: 1500 # # 25% of limit up to 2G # spike_limit_mib: 512 # check_interval: 5s # # # 50% of the maximum memory # limit_percentage: 50 # # 20% of max memory usage spike expected # spike_limit_percentage: 20 # queued_retry: # num_workers: 4 # queue_size: 100 # retry_on_failure: true extensions: health_check: endpoint: 0.0.0.0:13133 zpages: endpoint: 0.0.0.0:55679 pprof: endpoint: 0.0.0.0:1777 exporters: clickhousemetricswrite: endpoint: tcp://clickhouse:9000/?database=signoz_metrics service: telemetry: metrics: address: 0.0.0.0:8888 extensions: - health_check - zpages - pprof pipelines: metrics: receivers: [redis, prometheus] processors: [batch] exporters: [clickhousemetricswrite]

    A few things to note about the Redis receiver settings. The following settings are required:

    • endpoint (no default): The hostname and port of the Redis instance, separated by a colon.

    We have also set the collection_interval to 60s. The default value is 10s, but that can be an expensive operation.

    If you have set up your Redis instance with a password, you would need to include it in the configuration settings:

    receivers: redis: endpoint: "localhost:6379" collection_interval: 60s password: $REDIS_PASSWORD

    The password must match the password specified in the requirepass Redis server configuration option.

    Once the yaml configuration is done, you must restart your Docker containers to enable the OpenTelemetry Collector to scrape metrics from the Redis server.

    In the deploy folder, run the following command at your terminal to stop Docker containers:

    docker-compose -f docker/clickhouse-setup/docker-compose.yaml stop

    Then use the following command to restart the Docker containers:

    docker-compose -f docker/clickhouse-setup/docker-compose.yaml up

    Once the containers are running again, you can use the Dashboards tab of SigNoz to create customized charts for monitoring your Redis instance.

    Monitoring Redis instance with SigNoz dashboards

    Click on the Dashboards and then + New Dashboards. A dashboard is composed of panels in SigNoz.

    You can create two different types of panels: Time Series and Value, based on your use case.

    Once you click on + Add panel , you will have access to a DIY metrics query builder to create the charts that you need.

    You can also use native ClickHouse queries or PromQL for creating the charts, but the query builder provides the easiest experience.

    Once you are done building your charts, you can save the layout and start monitoring your Redis instance.

    Full-stack APM experience for Redis

    SigNoz is a full-stack open source APM that can monitor application statistics like latency, error rate, and requests per second with out-of-box charts and visualization.

    If you’re using Redis in your technology stack, SigNoz APM can capture your application interaction with Redis. SigNoz also provides distributed tracing. With Flamegraphs and Gantt charts, you can visualize Redis calls as part of the entire transaction.

    Final thoughts: end-to-end visibility of Redis

    In this post, we learned how to set up Redis monitoring with SigNoz using OpenTelemetry collectors. Modern applications are mostly distributed systems. Performance monitoring of such systems requires complete visibility into each component and its interactions. A full stack APM like SigNoz can give you end-to-end visibility into your systems.

    OpenTelemetry can instrument a wide variety of technologies. The data pipeline of OpenTelemetry Collector makes OpenTelemetry an ideal integration with other tools in the ecosystem. It also provides clients libraries for instrumenting application code in all the major programming languages.

    OpenTelemetry and SigNoz make an ideal combo to monitor applications that use Redis in their stack. Visit our GitHub repo and get started with Redis monitoring:

    If you want to know more about SigNoz, read this blog:

    SigNoz - an open source alternative to DataDog

    If you want to understand more about OpenTelemetry Collector, read this blog:

    OpenTelemetry Collector - complete guide

    Once suspended, signoz will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

    Once unsuspended, signoz will be able to comment and publish posts again.

    Once unpublished, all posts by signoz will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

    If signoz is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.
item.author: ankit01oss

item.title: 涉足不安全代码 | Dipping Toes into Unsafe Code
item.description:
不安全的代码：每个程序员的祸根。 对于 Rust 和 C# 等语言， unsafe的代码在语言级别与其他代码正式隔离开来。 尽管本质上是“不安全的”，但它们通常是大多数“安全”抽象的基础。

毫不奇怪，不安全的代码以难以处理而闻名。 边缘情况、讨厌的陷阱、资源损坏和不变的违规困扰着原本无所不能的语言功能。

在本文中，我们将尝试在 Rust 中实现我们自己对不安全代码的安全抽象。 在此过程中，我们探索了维护安全时所涉及的心态和思维过程。

一个选项块

作为我们的激励示例，我们实现了一个内存高效的直接地址表 (DAT)。 可以将 DAT 视为哈希映射的一种特殊情况，其中：

  • 键是一个整数。
  • 该值可以是任何类型T 。
  • 哈希函数就是恒等函数。 一个简单的表示

    我们可能很想根据[Option<T>; N] 但是，由于超出本文范围2的原因，可选值数组是内存效率低下的。 在最坏的情况下， [Option<T>; N] 因此，它由两部分数据组成：有效负载和判别/标签。

    长话短说，大多数数据类型（尤其是那些用户定义的）要求enum判别式在包装在Option中时与内部数据分开存储（从而增加内存占用）。 事实上，这通常适用于所有携带数据的enum类型，而不仅仅是Option 。

    这条规则只有少数例外。 即，这些包括但不限于引用（ &T和&mut T ）、智能指针（ Box<T> 、 Rc<T> 、 Arc<T>等），以及整数的非零变体类型（ NonZeroU8 ， NonZeroU16等）。 从上面的例子中，很明显[Unoptimized; N] 这恰好是最坏的情况，其中一位判别式浪费了太多空间。

    另一种表示

    幸运的是，并不是所有的希望都消失了！ 我们不使用可选值数组，而是将（可能未初始化的）值存储在数组中（按原样）。 然后，我们通过整数位掩码跟踪初始化值。 这样做，我们保证所有的判别式最多占用一位。 这比以前的表示要好，因为我们不再在显式判别器上浪费位。

     // A neat wrapper that marks data as "maybe-uninitialized". use core :: mem :: MaybeUninit ; // A block of 32 optional values. pub struct Block32 < T > { // An array of 32 "maybe-uninitialized" values. data : [ MaybeUninit < T > ; 32 ], // Since we have 32 values, we use an // unsigned 32-bit integer as our mask. // The least significant bit keeps track // of the discriminant at index `0`. mask : u32 , }

    最值得注意的是，我们的新表示使用“可能未初始化”值的数组。 当（且仅当）它是 DAT 中的有效条目时，基础数组中的值被初始化。 标准库中的core::mem::MaybeUninit包装器简化了这个用例。

    关于可能未初始化的内存

    在我们继续实施之前，我们必须首先查阅MaybeUninit的文档，了解我们必须坚持的任何安全不变量。 下面列出了最相关的功能和安全措施：

    1. 类似地， assume_init_ref方法将&MaybeUninit<T>转换为&T 。
    2. 同时， assume_init_mut方法将&mut MaybeUninit<T>转换为&mut T 。
  • 在调用任何assume_init初始化方法之前，必须正确初始化底层内部类型T
    • 否则，由于使用了垃圾内存，行为未定义。
  • 默认情况下， MaybeUninit<T> （原样）不会删除内部T ——即使内部值已正确初始化！
    • 由于T可能未初始化，我们不能假设在潜在的“垃圾内存”上调用析构函数是安全的。
    • 标准库采取了一种保守的方法，根本不调用析构函数。 因此，实际上很容易泄漏内存！
    • 有两种方法可以删除内部值：
      • 调用assume_init_drop方法，该方法接受一个&mut MaybeUninit<T>并在原地删除内部T
      • 在拥有的MaybeUninit<T>上调用assume_init方法并简单地删除结果T （通过离开当前范围）。
    •  use core :: mem :: MaybeUninit ; // IMPORTANT: Accessing garbage memory is undefined behavior! let uninit = MaybeUninit :: < u32 > :: uninit (); let _ = unsafe { uninit .assume_init () }; // Garbage Memory! // IMPORTANT: Improperly initialized values invoke undefined behavior! // Recall that references can never be null. let null_ref = MaybeUninit :: <& u32 > :: zeroed (); let _ = unsafe { null_ref .assume_init () }; // Invalid Initialization! // IMPORTANT: `MaybeUninit` (as is) does not invoke destructors. let valid_string = MaybeUninit :: new ( String :: from ( "Hello!" )); drop ( valid_string ); // Memory Leak!

    实现一些基本操作

    我们现在可以实现 DAT。 首先，我们定义一个默认构造函数。

     #![feature(maybe_uninit_uninit_array)] impl < T > Default for Block32 < T > { fn default () -> Self { Self { // As of Rust 1.62, note that the `uninit_array` method // is a nightly-only feature. For the sake of brevity, // we will be using this function. // // Alternatively, for stable toolchains, we may opt to // directly inline the definition for `uninit_array` // instead. Please consult the documentation accordingly. data : MaybeUninit :: uninit_array (), // We then start with an empty mask. mask : 0 , } } }

    然后，我们继续进行简单、安全的功能。 接下来，我们实现insert和remove方法。 由于这些方法与MaybeUninit交互，我们需要更加小心不可避免的unsafe代码。

    为所有unsafe代码的使用添加“安全注释”是最佳实践（理想情况下不仅仅是在 Rust 中）。 顾名思义，安全注释应该解释为什么调用unsafe的过程是安全的。 通常，它阐明了在程序之前和之后维护的（内部和外部）不变量。

     use core :: mem :: replace ; impl < T > Block32 < T > { // Inserts a `value` at `index`. Returns the old value, if any. pub fn insert ( & mut self , index : usize , value : T ) -> Option < T > { // Extract the old value let wrapped = MaybeUninit :: new ( value ); let old_vacant = self .is_vacant ( index ); let old_value = replace ( & mut self .data [ index ], wrapped ); // Ensure that the bit in the mask is set to 1 self .mask | = 1 << index ; // Return the old value if old_vacant { None } else { // SAFETY: Old value has been verified // to be properly initialized. Some ( unsafe { old_value .assume_init () }) } } // Removes a `value` at `index`. Returns the old value, if any. pub fn remove ( & mut self , index : usize ) -> Option < T > { if self .is_vacant ( index ) { return None ; } // Extract the old value let dest = & mut self .data [ index ]; let old_value = replace ( dest , MaybeUninit :: uninit ()); // Ensure that the bit in the mask is reset to 0 self .mask &= ! ( 1 << index ); // SAFETY: We have verified that the slot is not vacant. Some ( unsafe { old_value .assume_init () }) } }

    最后，我们实现 getter 方法。 这些相对更直接，因为我们不必考虑改变内部位掩码。

     impl < T > Block32 < T > { // Immutably borrow the value at `index`. pub fn get ( & self , index : usize ) -> Option <& T > { if self .is_vacant ( index ) { None } else { // SAFETY: Slot is initialized. Some ( unsafe { self .data [ index ] .assume_init_ref () }) } } // Mutably borrow the value at `index`. pub fn get_mut ( & mut self , index : usize ) -> Option <& mut T > { if self .is_vacant ( index ) { None } else { // SAFETY: Slot is initialized. Some ( unsafe { self .data [ index ] .assume_init_mut () }) } } }

    实施Clone

    我们现在必须允许用户随意克隆 DAT。 不幸的是，我们不能在我们的实现中简单地derive(Clone) 。 假设内部类型T实现Copy ，则Clone实现非常简单。

     // `T` must implement `Copy`... // 👇 impl < T : Copy > Clone for Block32 < T > { fn clone ( & self ) -> Self { Self { // Recall that `MaybeUninit<T>` implements `Copy` if // (and only if) `T` implements `Copy` as well. // Therefore, a bitwise copy of an array of `T` values // should be trivial. // // There are no extra lifetime considerations here since // `Copy` guarantees that `T` cannot implement a fancy // `Drop` implementation (stated in the documentation). data : self .data , mask : self .mask , } } }

    然而，这种方法有一个明显的问题。 类型实现Copy是相当罕见的！ 理想情况下，当（且仅当）内部类型T也实现Clone时，我们希望为 DAT 实现Clone 。 要求Copy太严格了！

    由于我们将Copy绑定为只是Clone ，因此data的按位副本不再正确。 我们必须将data中的每个T显式克隆到新的 DAT 中，以便我们调用任何额外的（但必要的）克隆逻辑。 对于大多数类型，按位复制就足够了； 对于其他人（例如String ），额外的清理和簿记是必要的。

     // `T` is only required to implement `Clone` (not `Copy`). // This should be less restrictive and hence applicable to // more types... // 👇 impl < T : Clone > Clone for Block32 < T > { fn clone ( & self ) -> Self { let mut block = Self :: default (); block .mask = self .mask ; for i in 0 .. 32 { // Do not clone vacant slots if self .is_vacant ( i ) { continue ; } // SAFETY: Slot is not vacant, and hence initialized. let value = unsafe { self .data [ i ] .assume_init_ref () }; // Manually clone the data block .data [ i ] = MaybeUninit :: new ( value .clone ()); } block } }

    不好了！ 潜在的内存泄漏！

    现在我们需要实现最后一个重要功能。 但首先，我们必须证明这个问题。 考虑以下代码片段。

     let mut block = Block32 :: default (); block .insert ( 0 , String :: from ( "Hello" )); block .insert ( 1 , String :: from ( "World" )); block .insert ( 2 , String :: from ( "Rusty" )); block .remove ( 2 ); drop ( block ); // Leaks "Hello" and "World"!

    这个看似无辜的代码片段实际上泄漏了很多内存！ 回想一下， MaybeUninit （原样）从不调用析构函数。 要正确删除该值，我们必须首先调用假设初始化将assume_init MaybeUninit<T>为T 。 转换为T可以应用常规丢弃规则。

    回到示例， insert和remove操作没有问题。 由于它们都在内部调用assume_init ，因此每个String在移出block时都会正确删除。

    当block本身超出范围时会发生内存泄漏，但底层数组中仍有一些已初始化的元素。 由于没有运行析构函数，内存肯定会泄漏。 因此，解决方案是为我们的 DAT 实施Drop ，以便正确销毁任何剩余的元素。

     impl < T > Drop for Block32 < T > { fn drop ( & mut self ) { for i in 0 .. 32 { // No more memory leaks! An `Option<T>` is dropped // here, which implies dropping the inner `T`, if any. self .remove ( i ); } } }

    改进的内存占用

    use core ::{ mem :: size_of , num :: NonZeroU8 }; // 64 Bytes type Unoptimized = [ Option < u8 > ; 32 ]; assert_eq! ( size_of :: < Unoptimized > (), 32 * 2 ); // 32 Bytes type Optimized = [ Option < NonZeroU8 > ; 32 ]; assert_eq! ( size_of :: < Optimized > (), 32 ); // 36 Bytes assert_eq! ( size_of :: < Block32 < u8 >> (), 32 + 4 );

    在最坏的情况下，原始的“可选值数组”表示需要 64 字节的内存。 为 32 个条目中的每一个分配两个字节：一个字节用于实际的u8有效负载，另一个字节用于enum判别式。 这占用的字节数是必要的两倍！

    同时，最好的情况（使用NonZeroU8 ）只需要 32 字节的内存。 不再需要存储enum判别式，这将内存占用减少了一半！ 不幸的是，大多数类型无论如何都没有资格进行这种优化。

    对于这种情况，我们的 DAT 实现提供了与最佳情况相当接近的内存占用。 强制位掩码只有（可以说）可以忽略不计的开销，但它比未优化的版本要好得多。

    结论

    老实说，我很想通过断言unsafe代码并不像看起来那么可怕来结束这篇文章。 然而，正如我们所看到的，有很多东西需要考虑——即使对于我们相对简单的MaybeUninit示例也是如此！ 通过实现 DAT，我们触及了诸如未初始化内存、析构函数、按位复制和显式克隆等主题。

    考虑到实现低级数据结构是多么笨拙，人们可能会想完全放弃unsafe的代码。 但是……这才是重点。 编写unsafe的代码是笨拙的，因为它本质上是正确的！ 隔离unsafe代码的语言恳求我们更仔细地思考。

    看到我们的代码编辑器突出显示unsafe关键字可能会令人生畏，但只要后面有合理的安全注释，我们就可以放心，事先已经进行了仔细考虑。 当然，这并不是说所有unsafe的程序员都是万无一失的。 如果确实发生了不安全错误，我们确切地知道去哪里寻找，这不是大多数语言提供的保证。

    将所有内容联系在一起的共同点是文档。 不变量的清晰文档补充了安全抽象和不安全低级操作之间的语言级分界。 理想情况下，它为我们提供了一个不变量的心理清单，可以像某种 Unsafe Bingo一样坚持。 核对清单完成后，我们可以以合理的信心断言安全。

    正如我们在 Rust 的文档中看到的那样，可以访问unsafe的代码。 文字当然不是微不足道的，但也不一定是吓人的。 尽管在大多数情况下我们必须更喜欢安全的抽象，但清晰的文档使我们能够在需要unsafe的代码时亲自动手。

    奖励： option-block板条箱

    形式上讲，它是以固定大小的数组为存储介质的直接地址表。

    重要的是，不要与流行的slab crate 混淆，后者在内部使用动态大小、堆分配的Vec 。 尽管这两个 crate 都提供索引访问和类似地图的功能，但option-block在较低级别上运行。

    具体来说， option-block在插入时不会跟踪分配中的下一个空槽（与slab不同）。 相反， option-block只是一个数组和位掩码的包装器。 该数组包含（可能未初始化的）数据，而位掩码跟踪分配中的有效（即已初始化）条目。 同样，它基本上是一个直接地址表。

    此板条箱与no_std环境兼容！ 例子

    let mut block = option_block :: Block8 :: <
    …

    为方便起见，我发布了option-block crate ，其中包括我们在本文中实现的固定大小的选项块的 8、16、32、64 和 128 元素变体。 这些变体分别命名为Block8 、 Block16 、 Block32 、 Block64和Block128 。

    这些特性包括迭代器4和便捷获取器（ get_or 、 get_or_else和get_or_default ）。

    话虽如此，请随时提出问题并提交拉取请求！

    1. 换句话说，映射的键实际上是底层数组存储的索引。 请参阅可空指针优化。 请注意，所有这些类型都有一个共同点：判别式由值隐式编码。 引用永远不会无效，因此编译器将None::<&T>编码为空指针。 同样的推理也适用于Box<T> ，它也永远不会为空。 类似地，对于非零整数，文字零用于编码None变体。 由于判别式是隐式的，编译器通过排除判别式开销来优化内存表示。 在撰写本文时，我还没有实现iter_mut方法。 ↩ ↩



    原文:

    Unsafe code: the bane of every programmer. For languages like Rust and C#, unsafe code is formally quarantined from the rest of the code at the language level. Although inherently "unsafe", they often serve as the foundation of most "safe" abstractions.

    Unsurprisingly, unsafe code has a reputation for being tricky to deal with. Edge cases, nasty pitfalls, resource corruption, and invariant violations plague an otherwise omnipotent language feature.

    In this article, we dip our toes into implementing our own safe abstraction over unsafe code in Rust. In doing so, we explore the mindset and thought processes involved when upholding safety.

    A Block of Optionals

    As our motivating example, we implement a memory-efficient direct-address table (DAT). One can think of DATs as a special case of hash maps where:

    • The key is an integer.
    • The value can be any type T.
    • The hash function is simply the identity function.1

    A Simple Representation

    We may be tempted to implement a DAT in terms of [Option<T>; N], where N is a usize representing the number of entries in the DAT. However, for reasons beyond the scope of this article2, an array of optional values is memory-inefficient. In the worst-case scenario, the [Option<T>; N] representation requires twice as much memory as the DAT we will be implementing.

    RECALL: The Option type—like most data-carrying enum types in Rust—are essentially tagged unions. It is thus composed of two pieces of data: the payload and the discriminant/tag.

    To make a long story short, most data types (especially those user-defined) require the enum discriminant to be stored separately from the inner data when wrapped in an Option (thereby increasing the memory footprint). In fact, this is generally true for all data-carrying enum types, not just for Option.

    There are only a few exceptions to this rule. Namely, these include—but are not limited to—references (&T and &mut T), smart pointers (Box<T>, Rc<T>, Arc<T>, etc.), and the non-zero variants of the integer types (NonZeroU8, NonZeroU16, etc.).3

    use core::{mem::size_of, num::NonZeroU32}; // We obtain 8 bytes here: 4 bytes for the actual `u32`
// plus 4 more bytes for the `enum` discriminant (which
// has been padded for alignment).
type Unoptimized = Option<u32>;
assert_eq!(size_of::<Unoptimized>(), 8); // We obtain 4 bytes here. There is no need to explicitly
// store a discriminant because the value itself implicitly
// encodes it. That is, the "zero" value is treated as the
// `None` variant. Otherwise, the value is in the `Some` variant.
type Optimized = Option<NonZeroU32>;
assert_eq!(size_of::<Optimized>(), 4);

    From the example above, it should be apparent that the [Unoptimized; N] representation takes up twice as much memory as the [Optimized; N] representation for any size N. This happens to be the worst-case scenario, where the one-bit discriminant wastes too much space.

    An Alternative Representation

    Fortunately, not all hope is lost! Instead of using an array of optional values, we store the (potentially uninitialized) values in an array (as is). Then, we keep track of the initialized values via an integer bit mask. In doing so, we guarantee that all discriminants occupy at most one bit. This is better than the previous representation because we no longer waste bits on explicit discriminants.

    // A neat wrapper that marks data as "maybe-uninitialized".
use core::mem::MaybeUninit; // A block of 32 optional values.
pub struct Block32<T> { // An array of 32 "maybe-uninitialized" values. data: [MaybeUninit<T>; 32], // Since we have 32 values, we use an // unsigned 32-bit integer as our mask. // The least significant bit keeps track // of the discriminant at index `0`. mask: u32,
}

    Most notably, our new representation uses an array of "maybe-uninitialized" values. A value in the underlying array is initialized if (and only if) it is a valid entry in the DAT. The core::mem::MaybeUninit wrapper from the standard library facilitates this use case.

    On Maybe-Uninitialized Memory

    Before we proceed with the implementation, we must first consult the documentation for MaybeUninit regarding any safety invariants we must uphold. The most relevant features and safety measures are listed below:

    1. The MaybeUninit::<T>::assume_init method casts a MaybeUninit<T> to a T.
      • Analogously, the assume_init_ref method casts a &MaybeUninit<T> to a &T.
      • Meanwhile, the assume_init_mut method casts a &mut MaybeUninit<T> to a &mut T.
    2. The underlying inner type T must be properly initialized before invoking any of the assume_init methods.
      • Otherwise, the behavior is undefined due to usage of garbage memory.
    3. By default, MaybeUninit<T> (as is) does not drop the inner T—even if the inner value is properly initialized!
      • Since T may be uninitialized, we cannot assume that it is safe to call the destructor on potentially "garbage memory".
      • The standard library takes a conservative approach by not invoking the destructor at all. Thus, it is actually easy to leak memory!
      • There are two ways to drop the inner value:
        • Invoke the assume_init_drop method, which takes in a &mut MaybeUninit<T> and drops the inner T in-place.
        • Invoke the assume_init method on an owned MaybeUninit<T> and simply drop the resultant T (by leaving the current scope). 
    use core::mem::MaybeUninit; // IMPORTANT: Accessing garbage memory is undefined behavior!
let uninit = MaybeUninit::<u32>::uninit();
let _ = unsafe { uninit.assume_init() }; // Garbage Memory! // IMPORTANT: Improperly initialized values invoke undefined behavior!
// Recall that references can never be null.
let null_ref = MaybeUninit::<&u32>::zeroed();
let _ = unsafe { null_ref.assume_init() }; // Invalid Initialization! // IMPORTANT: `MaybeUninit` (as is) does not invoke destructors.
let valid_string = MaybeUninit::new(String::from("Hello!"));
drop(valid_string); // Memory Leak!

    Implementing Some Basic Operations

    We may now implement the DAT. First, we define a default constructor.

    #![feature(maybe_uninit_uninit_array)] impl<T> Default for Block32<T> { fn default() -> Self { Self { // As of Rust 1.62, note that the `uninit_array` method // is a nightly-only feature. For the sake of brevity, // we will be using this function. // // Alternatively, for stable toolchains, we may opt to // directly inline the definition for `uninit_array` // instead. Please consult the documentation accordingly. data: MaybeUninit::uninit_array(), // We then start with an empty mask. mask: 0, } }
}

    Then, we proceed to the easy, safe functions. The is_vacant method is particularly important because it will be extensively used for upholding safety.

    impl<T> Block32<T> { // Checks whether the DAT is empty. pub const fn is_empty(&self) -> bool { self.mask == 0 } // Returns the number of valid entries in the DAT. pub const fn len(&self) -> u32 { self.mask.count_ones() } // Checks whether the slot at the `index` contains a // valid entry. Panics if the `index` is out of bounds. pub const fn is_vacant(&self, index: usize) -> bool { assert!(index < 32); let query = 1 << index; self.mask & query == 0 }
}

    Next, we implement the insert and remove methods. Since these methods interact with MaybeUninit, we need to be a bit more careful with the inevitable unsafe code.

    It is best practice (ideally not just in Rust) to add a "safety comment" for all uses of unsafe code. As its name suggests, safety comments should explain why it is safe to invoke an unsafe procedure. Typically, it clarifies the (internal and external) invariants that are upheld before and after the procedure.

    use core::mem::replace; impl<T> Block32<T> { // Inserts a `value` at `index`. Returns the old value, if any. pub fn insert(&mut self, index: usize, value: T) -> Option<T> { // Extract the old value let wrapped = MaybeUninit::new(value); let old_vacant = self.is_vacant(index); let old_value = replace(&mut self.data[index], wrapped); // Ensure that the bit in the mask is set to 1 self.mask |= 1 << index; // Return the old value if old_vacant { None } else { // SAFETY: Old value has been verified // to be properly initialized. Some(unsafe { old_value.assume_init() }) } } // Removes a `value` at `index`. Returns the old value, if any. pub fn remove(&mut self, index: usize) -> Option<T> { if self.is_vacant(index) { return None; } // Extract the old value let dest = &mut self.data[index]; let old_value = replace(dest, MaybeUninit::uninit()); // Ensure that the bit in the mask is reset to 0 self.mask &= !(1 << index); // SAFETY: We have verified that the slot is not vacant. Some(unsafe { old_value.assume_init() }) }
}

    Finally, we implement the getter methods. These are relatively more straightforward because we do not have to think about mutating the internal bit mask.

    impl<T> Block32<T> { // Immutably borrow the value at `index`. pub fn get(&self, index: usize) -> Option<&T> { if self.is_vacant(index) { None } else { // SAFETY: Slot is initialized. Some(unsafe { self.data[index].assume_init_ref() }) } } // Mutably borrow the value at `index`. pub fn get_mut(&mut self, index: usize) -> Option<&mut T> { if self.is_vacant(index) { None } else { // SAFETY: Slot is initialized. Some(unsafe { self.data[index].assume_init_mut() }) } }
}

    Implementing Clone

    We must now enable to user to clone the DAT as they please. Unfortunately, we cannot simply derive(Clone) in our implementation. Assuming that the inner type T implements Copy, the Clone implementation is surprisingly trivial.

    // `T` must implement `Copy`...
// 👇
impl<T: Copy> Clone for Block32<T> { fn clone(&self) -> Self { Self { // Recall that `MaybeUninit<T>` implements `Copy` if // (and only if) `T` implements `Copy` as well. // Therefore, a bitwise copy of an array of `T` values // should be trivial. // // There are no extra lifetime considerations here since // `Copy` guarantees that `T` cannot implement a fancy // `Drop` implementation (stated in the documentation). data: self.data, mask: self.mask, } }
}

    However, there is one glaring issue with this approach. It is considerably uncommon for types to implement Copy! Ideally, we want to implement Clone for our DAT if (and only if) the inner type T implements Clone as well. Requiring Copy is just too restrictive!

    Since we are relaxing the Copy bound to be just Clone, then a bitwise copy of the data is no longer correct. We must explicitly clone each T in data into the new DAT so that we invoke any extra (but necessary) cloning logic. For most types, a bitwise copy is sufficient; for others (such as String), extra cleanup and bookkeeping is necessary.

    // `T` is only required to implement `Clone` (not `Copy`).
// This should be less restrictive and hence applicable to
// more types...
// 👇
impl<T: Clone> Clone for Block32<T> { fn clone(&self) -> Self { let mut block = Self::default(); block.mask = self.mask; for i in 0..32 { // Do not clone vacant slots if self.is_vacant(i) { continue; } // SAFETY: Slot is not vacant, and hence initialized. let value = unsafe { self.data[i].assume_init_ref() }; // Manually clone the data block.data[i] = MaybeUninit::new(value.clone()); } block }
}

    Oh, no! Potential memory leaks!

    Now there is one last important feature we need to implement. But first, we must demonstrate the problem. Consider the following code snippet.

    let mut block = Block32::default();
block.insert(0, String::from("Hello"));
block.insert(1, String::from("World"));
block.insert(2, String::from("Rusty"));
block.remove(2);
drop(block); // Leaks "Hello" and "World"!

    This seemingly innocent code snippet actually leaks a lot of memory! Recall that MaybeUninit (as is) never invokes the destructor. To properly drop the value, we must first invoke assume_init to cast the MaybeUninit<T> as a T. The conversion to T enables the regular drop rules to apply.

    Returning to the example, there are no issues with the insert and remove operations. Since they both invoke assume_init internally, each String is dropped properly when moved out of the block.

    The memory leak happens when the block itself goes out of scope, but there are still some initialized elements left in the underlying array. Since no destructors are run, memory is certainly leaked. The solution, then, is to implement Drop for our DAT so that any remaining elements are properly destroyed.

    impl<T> Drop for Block32<T> { fn drop(&mut self) { for i in 0..32 { // No more memory leaks! An `Option<T>` is dropped // here, which implies dropping the inner `T`, if any. self.remove(i); } }
}

    Improved Memory Footprint 

    use core::{mem::size_of, num::NonZeroU8}; // 64 Bytes
type Unoptimized = [Option<u8>; 32];
assert_eq!(size_of::<Unoptimized>(), 32 * 2); // 32 Bytes
type Optimized = [Option<NonZeroU8>; 32];
assert_eq!(size_of::<Optimized>(), 32); // 36 Bytes
assert_eq!(size_of::<Block32<u8>>(), 32 + 4);

    In the worst-case scenario, the original "array-of-optional-values" representation requires 64 bytes of memory. Two bytes are allocated for each of the 32 entries: one byte for the actual u8 payload and another byte for the enum discriminant. This takes up twice as many bytes than necessary!

    Meanwhile, the best-case scenario (using NonZeroU8) requires only 32 bytes of memory. Storing the enum discriminant is no longer necessary, which cuts the memory footprint by half! Unfortunately, most types are not eligible for this optimization anyway.

    For such cases, our DAT implementation provides a reasonably close memory footprint to that of the best-case scenario. There is only the (arguably) negligible overhead that comes with the mandatory bit mask, which is nevertheless significantly better than the unoptimized version.

    Conclusion

    To be honest, I would love to conclude this article by asserting that unsafe code is not as scary as it seems. As we have seen, though, there is a lot to consider—even for our relatively simple example with MaybeUninit! By implementing a DAT, we have touched topics like uninitialized memory, destructors, bitwise copies, and explicit clones.

    One may be tempted to scrap unsafe code altogether considering how unwieldly it is to implement low-level data structures. But... that's actually the point. Writing unsafe code is unwieldly because it is inherently tricky to get right! Languages that isolate unsafe code implore us to think more carefully.

    It may be intimidating to see our code editors highlight the unsafe keyword, but as long as there are reasonable safety comments that follow, we may rest assured that careful considerations have taken place beforehand. Of course, this is not to say that all unsafe programmers are infallible. If an unsafety bug does occur, we know exactly where to look, which is not an assurance that most languages offer.

    The common thread that ties everything together is documentation. Clear documentation of invariants supplement the language-level demarcation between safe abstractions and unsafe low-level operations. Ideally, it gives us a mental checklist of invariants to uphold like some kind of Unsafe Bingo. When the checklist is done, we may assert safety with a reasonable degree of confidence.

    As we have seen with Rust's documentation, unsafe code can be accessible. The text is certainly not trivial, but it does not have to be scary. Although we must prefer safe abstractions in most cases, clear documentation empowers us to get our hands dirty when unsafe code becomes necessary.

    Bonus: The option-block Crate

    The option-block crate provides a simple primitive for fixed-size blocks of optional types. Formally speaking, it's a direct-address table with a fixed-size array as the storage medium.

    Importantly, this is not to be confused with the popular slab crate, which internally uses the dynamically-sized, heap-allocated Vec. Although both crates provide indexed accesses and map-like features, option-block operates at a lower level.

    Specifically, option-block does not keep track of the next empty slot in the allocation upon insertion (unlike slab). Instead, option-block is simply a wrapper around an array and a bit mask. The array contains the (maybe uninitialized) data while the bit mask keeps track of the valid (i.e. initialized) entries in the allocation. Again, it's basically a direct-address table.

    This crate is compatible with no_std environments! Neither std nor alloc is necessary.

    Example

    let mut block = option_block::Block8::<
    …

    For your convenience, I published the option-block crate, which includes the 8-, 16-, 32-, 64-, and 128-element variants of the fixed-size block of optionals we have implemented in this article. These variants are respectively named Block8, Block16, Block32, Block64, and Block128.

    The crate also adds some extra methods that make our DAT's public API more compatible with the standard collections. Among these features are iterators4 and convenience getters (get_or, get_or_else, and get_or_default).

    With that said, please feel free to raise issues and submit pull requests!

    1. In other words, the key to the map is literally an index into the underlying array storage. ↩

    2. See the nullable pointer optimization. ↩

    3. Observe that all of these types have one thing in common: the discriminant is implicitly encoded by the value. References can never be invalid, so the compiler encodes None::<&T> as the null pointer. The same reasoning applies to Box<T>, which are also never null. Similarly, for non-zero integers, the literal zero is used to encode the None variant. Since the discriminant is implicit, the compiler optimizes the memory representation by excluding the discriminant overhead. ↩

    4. As of writing, I have not yet implemented the iter_mut method. ↩
item.author: somedood

item.title: 如何在 GitHub 上获得 2000+ 星以及 11 个地方来推广您的项目 | How to get 2000+ stars on GitHub with 11 places to promote your project
item.description:
我的带有 Next.js 的 React Boilerplate最近在 GitHub 上获得了 2000 多颗星。 在过去的几个月里，我一直致力于改进项目并添加新功能。 不幸的是，这还不足以让社区注意到该项目。 我还花时间在许多平台上推广该项目，在本文中，我将与您分享我推广开源项目的 11 个地方。

为成功的促销做准备

在推广您的项目之前，您需要确保其他开发人员会对它感兴趣。 就我而言，作为一名独立制作者，我不断开发新产品。 因此，我一次又一次地浪费时间安装和配置新的 React 项目。 例如，我花时间配置 TypeScript、ESLint、Prettier、Jest、Cypress 和其他与 JavaScript 相关的工具。

带着这种挫败感，我决定创建一个 React 样板，您可以使用它来快速启动一个新项目。 我以为其他人也会有同样的痛苦。 所以，我把这个项目开源了，每个人都可以访问。

您还需要确保您的项目易于使用。 您提供的任何文档、教程或自述文件都将帮助其他开发人员入门。 尤其是如果你有一个好看的 README 文件，它肯定会增加你在 GitHub 项目中获得良好第一印象和加星的机会。

确保一切就绪后，我们就可以开始在这11个地方进行推广了。

对于我的 React Boilerplate，该项目目前在 Google 上的关键字Next.js Boilerplate 。 它为存储库带来了大量流量，并增加了 GitHub 上的星数。 实际上，该项目从谷歌获得的流量最多：谷歌带来的流量比 GitHub 本身还多。 这就是为什么编写 README 文件以供 Google 索引非常重要的原因。

然后，您的项目会自动从 Google 获得一些流量。 完全是被动的。 但是，搜索引擎优化既困难又复杂，尤其是当您针对竞争激烈的关键字时。 有很多因素会影响您的 Google 排名。 它需要您投入时间来了解 SEO 策略以及如何实施它。

如您所知，Twitter 拥有大量可能对您的项目感兴趣的开发人员。 如果您有大量的追随者，那么当您开始推广您的项目时，您应该不会有任何问题被注意到。

但是，当你没有很多像我这样的追随者时，你需要确保你有很好的机会被关注。 您应该在推文中添加一些主题标签，这将帮助您扩大覆盖面。 例如，您可以添加一些主题标签，例如用于构建开源项目的技术堆栈，例如#react、#reactjs、#javaScript、#webdev、#nextjs 等。

这是我的推文的一个例子，我得到了 40 个赞和 21 个转发（只有 100 个关注者）：

几乎每个技术堆栈都有社区。 例如，有一个名为/r/reactjs的 React 子版块。 而且，还有一个针对 Node.js、JavaScript、TypeScript、AWS 等的子版块。找到一个对推广您的项目有意义的社区不会有困难。

供您参考，每个子版块都有不同的规则和指南。 一些 subreddit 比其他 subreddit 更严格。 我建议您花一些时间阅读它们并确保您遵循它们。

对于我在 Reddit 上的一篇帖子，我得到了 XXX 点赞，这在 GitHub 上转化为很多星（我不记得确切的数字了）：

您可以使用Awesome Lists在 GitHub 上直接找到类似的列表。 对于软件工程中的每个技术堆栈和每个领域，几乎都有一个很棒的列表。 找到适合您项目的列表应该不难。 对于 Next.js 样板，我已发布到以下列表中：

  • 很棒的顺风 CSS
  • 很棒的 AWS 放大
  • 很棒的 JAMStack

有几个网站列出了使用特定技术堆栈的项目。 例如，BestOfJs 是一个聚合使用 JavaScript 构建的开源项目的网站。 您可以为 React、Vue、Tailwind CSS 等找到类似的网站。

并非 Indie Hacker 上的每个人都是开发人员。 因此，并不是每个人都会对您的开源项目感兴趣。 但是，Indie Hacker 上最大的群体之一是开发人员。 独立黑客的人正在建立一家初创公司或引导公司。 因此，如果您的项目可以帮助他们开展业务，您应该在 Indie Hacker 上推广它。

我的 React 样板模板可帮助开发人员开始一个新项目。 因此，独立制作者可以使用它来开始他们的新产品，无论是登陆页面、SaaS、博客等。这就是我在 Indie Hacker 上进行一些促销的原因。

如果有的话，你可以在你的博客上写一篇关于你的项目的博文。 因此，您可以分享您的愿景和想法。 这是推广您的项目的完美解决方案。 或者，您也可以编写有关如何使用项目的教程。

您也可以在 Dev.to、Hashnode、Medium 或任何其他平台上发帖作为替代。 或者，您可以将您的博客文章从您的个人博客重新发布到这些平台。

今天，大多数开源项目都有一个不和谐的频道，他们可以在其中与其他开发人员讨论。 从其他开发人员那里获得帮助的好方法，您可以与使用相同技术的其他人联系。 例如，有一个名为Reactiflux的不和谐频道供 React 开发人员使用。 如果你的项目使用 React，加入服务器并推广你的项目是有意义的。

您可以找到很多面向开发人员的新闻通讯。 例如，您可以找到一份名为JavaScript Weekly的面向 JavaScript 开发人员的时事通讯。 您可以明确地尝试与他们联系，看看他们是否有兴趣在他们的时事通讯中介绍您的项目。 就像在 Reddit 上一样，您绝对可以找到可以推广您的项目的时事通讯。 几乎每个技术堆栈和软件工程中的每个领域都有一个时事通讯。

如果你的项目在短时间内获得了大量的星星，它就可以在 GitHub 趋势上出现。 在出现在 GitHub Trends 之后，我的 React Boilerplate 一天就收到了 150 多颗星。 因此，这对于在您的项目中获得明星非常有效。

它会根据用户的兴趣自动向用户推荐托管在 GitHub 上的开源项目。 因此，通过添加描述和标签来填写您的项目详细信息非常重要。

您的项目将显示在仪表板的右侧：

它还将显示在他们的探索页面中：

我自己喜欢这个功能，并用它来发现我可以使用的项目。

结论

这些是我发布的关于 Next.js 样板模板的 11 个地方。 这是一段漫长的旅程，我花了将近 2 年的时间才达到这个数字。 希望这篇文章可以帮助您并缩短您在项目中获得明星的旅程。

在这个样板上工作了将近两年后，我注意到通过创建一个 SaaS 样板可以走得更远。 这是完全相同的想法，但适用于 SaaS 产品。 所以，我构建了一个React SaaS Boilerplate ，一个样板，你可以获得创建 SaaS 并获得第一个 MRR 所需的一切。

它将处理制作 SaaS 产品所需的所有功能：身份验证、支付、团队支持、仪表板、登录页面等。因此，您可以专注于使您的项目与众不同并快速启动您的 SaaS 的原因。



原文:

My React Boilerplate with Next.js has recently reached 2000+ stars on GitHub. In the last months, I've continuously been working on improving the project and adding new features. Unfortunately, this isn't enough to get the project noticed by the community. I've also spent my time promoting the project on many platforms and in this article, I'll share with you my 11 places to promote an open source project.

Preparation for a successful promotion

Before promoting your project, you need to make sure other developers will be interested in it. In my case, as an indie maker, I constantly build new products. So, I was losing my time installing and configuring new React projects again and again. For example, I was spending my time configuring TypeScript, ESLint, Prettier, Jest, Cypress and other JavaScript-related tools.

With this frustration, I've decided to create a React boilerplate that you can use to start quickly a new project. I thought other people would have the same pain. So, I made the project open source and accessible to everyone.

You also need to make sure your project is easy to use. Any documentation, tutorials or README you provide will help other developers to get started. Especially if you have a good-looking README file, it'll definitively increase your chances of getting a good first impression and a star on your GitHub project.

After making sure that everything is in place, we can start promoting in these 11 places.

1. Google with SEO

For my React Boilerplate, the project is currently ranked number 1 on Google for the keyword Next.js Boilerplate. It brings a lot of traffic to the repository and increases the number of stars on GitHub. Actually, the project got the most traffic from Google: Google brings more traffic than GitHub itself. That's why it's extremely important to write the README file for Google to index.

The good thing about SEO is that you only need to set it up once. Then, your project automatically gets some traffic from Google. It's fully passive. But, SEO is hard and complex, especially if you target a highly competitive keyword. There are a lot of factors that can influence your Google ranking. It requires you to invest your time to learn about the SEO strategy and how to implement it.

2. Twitter

As you already know, Twitter has a large number of developers that can be interested in your project. If you have a large based of followers, you shouldn't have any issues getting noticed when you start promoting your project.

But, when you don't have a lot of followers like me, you need to make sure you have a good chance to get noticed. You should add some hashtags to your tweet, it'll help you to increase your reach. For example, you can add some hashtags like the technology stack you use to build your open source project like #react, #reactjs, #javaScript, #webdev, #nextjs, etc.

Here is one example of my tweet where I got 40 likes and 21 retweets (by having only 100 followers):

3. Reddit

Reddit has also a large community of programmers who can support you by giving you a star on your project. There are communities for almost every technology stack. For example, there is a subreddit for React named /r/reactjs. And, there is also a subreddit for Node.js, JavaScript, TypeScript, AWS, etc. You won't have difficulty finding a community where it makes sense to promote your project.

For your information, each subreddits has different rules and guidelines. Some subreddits are more strict than others. I suggest you take some time to read them and make sure you follow them.

For one of my posts on Reddit, I got XXX upvotes which translates to a lot of stars on GitHub (I don't remember the exact number):

4. Awesome lists

You can find a similar list directly on GitHub with Awesome Lists. There is almost an Awesome list for each technology stack and each field in software engineering. It shouldn't be hard to find a list that fits your project. For Next.js Boilerplate, I've posted into these lists:

  • Awesome Tailwind CSS
  • Awesome AWS Amplify
  • Awesome JAMStack

5. Publish aggregator websites

There are several websites that list projects using a specific technology stack. For example, BestOfJs is a website that aggregates open source projects built with JavaScript. You can find a similar website for React, Vue, Tailwind CSS, and more.

6. Indie Hacker

Not everybody on Indie Hacker is a developer. So, not everybody will be interested in your open-source project. But, one of the largest groups on Indie Hacker are developers. People on indie hackers are building a startup or bootstrap company. So if your project can help them in their business, you should promote it on Indie Hacker.

My React Boilerplate Template helps developers to start a new project. So, indie makers can use it to start their new productl, whether for a landing page, a SaaS, a blog, etc. That's why I've done some promotions on Indie Hacker.

7. Write a blog post

You can write a blog post about your project on your blog if you have one. So, you can share your vision and your idea. It's the perfect solution to promote your project. Or, you can also write a tutorial on how to use your project.

You can also post on Dev.to, Hashnode, Medium, or any other platform as an alternative. Or, you can republish your blog post from your personal blog into these platforms.

8. Discord

Today, most open source project has a discord channel where they can discuss with other developers. A great way to get help from other developers and you can connect with others who use the same technology. For example, there is a discord channel for React developers named Reactiflux. If your project uses React, it makes sense to join the server and promote your project.

9. Newsletter

You can find a lot of newsletters for developers. For example, you can find a newsletter for JavaScript developers called JavaScript Weekly. You can definitively try to contact them to see if they are interested in featuring your project in their newsletter. Like on Reddit, you can definitely find a newsletter where your project can be promoted. There is almost a newsletter for each technology stack and each field in software engineering.

10. GitHub Trends

Your project can be featured on GitHub Trends if it has received a large number of stars in a short period. After appearing in GitHub Trends, my React Boilerplate has received more than 150 stars in one day. So, this is super effective to get stars on your project.

11. Github Explore

GitHub Explore is a great way to find open source projects. It automatically suggests open-source projects hosted on GitHub to users based on their interests. So, it's important to fill your project details by adding description and tags.

Your project will show up on the right side of their dashboard:

It will also show up in their explore page:

I myself love this feature and use it to discover projects that I can use.

Conclusion

These are the 11 places I've posted about my Next.js Boilerplate Template. The project on GitHub has now 2000+ stars on GitHub. It was a long journey, it took me almost 2 years to reach this number. Hope this article can help you and shorten your journey to get stars on your project.

After working on this boilerplate for almost two years, I noticed it's possible to go further by creating a SaaS boilerplate. It's exactly the same idea but applies to a SaaS product. So, I build a React SaaS Boilerplate, a boilerplate you can get everything you need to create your SaaS and earn your 1st MRR.

It'll take care of all the features needed to make SaaS products: authentication, payment, team support, dashboard, landing page, etc. So, you focus on what makes your project unique and quickly launch your SaaS.
item.author: ixartz

item.title: 使用 Unity 构建多人游戏 | Building Multiplayer Games with Unity
item.description:
一些最有趣的游戏是您可以与朋友一起玩的游戏。 能够一起探索世界、打败怪物、克服谜题和挑战可能是令人惊叹的体验。

然而，在游戏中创建多人游戏功能可能是一项巨大的挑战。 创建允许客户可靠通信、作弊检测、处理网络问题等的架构需要大量的思考和规划。

在本博客中，我们将探讨多人游戏所需的许多注意事项，并展示一些使事情顺利和轻松运行的最佳方法。

介绍

为了最好地展示 Unity 中的多人游戏功能，而不是从头开始创建整个游戏，我联系了raywenderlich ，看看我是否可以用多人游戏功能扩展他们的塔防教程系列。 这是一个非常详细且内容丰富的塔防游戏教程，我强烈建议您查看它以充分了解这篇博文的背景。

有了这款核心单人游戏，我们为添加多人游戏功能奠定了坚实的基础。 我们将在本博客中介绍的核心功能和注意事项包括：

  • 如何沟通炮塔的放置和升级
  • 如何让客户与权威来源保持同步
  • 如何处理由于延迟、计算机问题等原因导致的客户端不同步
  • 如何处理客户端重新连接到游戏

我们将使用Unity进行开发，并使用Ably在设备之间实现可靠、可扩展的通信。

入门

首先，您需要安装 Unity。 最好的方法是从 Unity 网站获取Unity Hub ，然后从该网站安装最新版本的 Unity。 这最初是为 2020.3.28f1 创建的。

使用 Unity，从 GitHub 的starting-point分支上下载我们的基础项目，然后在 Unity 中加载它。 在开始任何事情之前，您需要调整游戏窗口的尺寸以使其正确呈现。 在主查看器中单击顶部的“游戏”选项卡，然后将纵横比更改为 4:3。

创建大厅

一个关键的事情是，由于这款游戏将是多人游戏，我们需要一种让玩家唯一识别游戏实例以加入、玩和观看的方法。 为此，我们将在实际游戏之前创建一个场景，玩家可以在其中输入他们想要加入的游戏的唯一 ID，然后单击按钮加载游戏。

在基本模板中，已经在Assets/Scenes中添加了一个名为Lobby的场景。 这是一个简单的场景，有一个文本输入，允许玩家输入他们想要加入的游戏（只要所有玩家输入相同的 ID，他们就会进入同一个游戏），以及一个提交按钮。

但是，目前，此按钮不起作用。 我们希望它运行一个脚本，该脚本将加载实际的游戏场景，并传达此处输入的唯一游戏 ID 以供游戏使用。

保持游戏状态

为了处理游戏 ID 持久性，我们可以创建一个新的 C# 脚本，我们将其称为StateManagerBehaviour 。 在此脚本中放置以下代码：

 using UnityEngine ; public class StateManagerBehaviour : MonoBehaviour { public static StateManagerBehaviour Instance ; public string GameID ; void Awake () { if ( Instance == null ) { Instance = this ; DontDestroyOnLoad ( gameObject ); } else if ( Instance != null ) { Destroy ( this . gameObject ); } } }

在这里，我们有一个脚本，它将阻止它所附加的任何东西在场景之间被破坏。 这意味着任何变量集，例如当前游戏的 ID，都可以放在此处并持久化。

在大厅场景中，创建一个 Empty GameObject 并将其命名为StateManager 。 将StateManagerBehaviour脚本附加到它。 这个对象和附加的脚本现在将在场景之间持续存在！

不断变化的场景

现在我们可以持久化一个游戏 ID，我们需要让提交按钮起作用。 该按钮需要：

  • 更新 StateManagerBehaviour 中GameID的值以匹配输入框中的内容
  • 加载游戏场景

为此，请创建一个附加到名为JoinGame的按钮的新脚本，并将以下代码添加到其中：

 using UnityEngine ; using UnityEngine.SceneManagement ; using UnityEngine.UI ; using TMPro ; public class JoinGame : MonoBehaviour { [ SerializeField ] private Button startButton ; [ SerializeField ] public TMP_InputField gameIDField ; void Start () { startButton . onClick . AddListener (() => { if ( gameIDField . text == "" ) return ; StateManagerBehaviour . Instance . GameID = gameIDField . text ; SceneManager . LoadScene ( "GameScene" ); }); } }

在 Hierarchy 中选择JoinGameButton ，然后将InputField拖放到JoinGame脚本的Game ID Field中，并将JoinGameButton本身拖放到Start Button字段中。

该按钮现在应该可以使用了！ 这应该意味着我们可以在构建项目时从这个大厅场景加载游戏场景！

然而，在测试它之前，我们需要检查两个场景是否都添加到了我们的构建设置中。 转到File -> Build Settings… ，并将两个场景添加到Scenes in Build section 。

这样，您可以尝试运行大厅场景，如果一切顺利，您应该可以输入游戏 ID 的值，然后点击按钮加载塔防游戏！

添加多人游戏功能

添加大厅后，我们可以开始研究如何包含多人游戏功能。 由于核心用户交互是放置怪物以击败成群的敌人，因此我们将首先研究客户端之间的通信。

对于我们的通信层，我们将使用 Ably。 在游戏中，每个客户端都会向 Ably 的一个服务器发送消息，而游戏中其他订阅更新的客户端将收到这些消息。

要使用 Ably，您需要注册一个免费帐户。 拥有帐户后，您可以从帐户仪表板获取 API 密钥，这将允许客户以编程方式使用 Ably。 在本节中，我们将在代码中使用 API 密钥，但您最终应该将其替换为令牌身份验证，以确保用户只能访问您可以完全控制的短期令牌授予的权限。

获得 Ably Unity 软件包

创建帐户并掌握 API 密钥后，我们现在需要获取 Ably 提供的客户端库之一，以便轻松与系统交互。 如果您访问Ably C# GitHub 存储库，您可以从发行版下载最新版本的 Unity 包。

在撰写本文时，最新版本是 1.2.8，可以在此处下载。

有了统一包，我们需要将它导入到我们的 Unity 项目中。 在顶部菜单中，选择Assets -> Import Package -> Custom Package… ，然后选择刚刚下载的 unitypackage。 在选项中，取消勾选 JsonNet 项目。 如果您忘记在这一步，只需删除将出现在 UnityPackages 目录中的文件夹。

如果您收到有关 JsonNet 版本错误的错误，请转到Edit - Project Settings 。 在这里转到Player选项卡，然后在“配置”下，“程序集版本验证”取消选中它。

在客户端之间共享炮塔升级

添加 Ably 库后，我们现在可以使用它在客户端之间共享添加的怪物。 由于我们将在多个脚本中使用 Ably，让我们创建一个对象来为我们保存 Ably 客户端库的单个实例。 在GameScene场景中，添加一个名为AblyManager的空游戏对象，并创建一个脚本以附加到它，名为AblyManagerBehaviour 。 在此脚本中，添加以下内容：

 using IO.Ably ; using IO.Ably.Realtime ; using UnityEngine ; public class AblyManagerBehaviour : MonoBehaviour { private AblyRealtime realtime = new AblyRealtime ( new ClientOptions { Key = "INSERT_ABLY_API_KEY_HERE" } ); public IRealtimeChannel gameChannel ; // Start is called before the first frame update void Awake () { gameChannel = realtime . Channels . Get ( StateManagerBehaviour . Instance . GameID ); } }

确保将INSERT_ABLY_API_KEY_HERE文本替换为您之前从帐户仪表板获得的 Ably API 密钥。

在这里，我们使用 API 密钥连接到 Ably 以对我们进行身份验证，然后实例化 Ably 通道。 它们由一个名称唯一标识，在本例中，指定为StateManagerBehaviour.Instance.GameID 。

我们使用之前指定的状态对象来确保只有希望参与由 GameID 标识的特定游戏的客户端才会使用 Ably 通道。

发送和接收消息

我们实例化的 Ably 实例将能够被我们的其他脚本用来在客户端之间进行通信。 打开PlaceMonster脚本，在类的顶部添加以下内容：

 private AblyManagerBehaviour ablyManager ;

我们可以在Start函数中实例化这个对 AblyManager 的引用：

 ablyManager = GameObject . Find ( "AblyManager" ). GetComponent < AblyManagerBehaviour >();

通过这个对 AblyManager 的引用，我们现在可以使用它的 Ably Channel。 我们需要的是每当玩家点击添加或升级怪物时发送消息，以便客户端能够自己订阅和表示这些操作。

发布玩家输入

为此，我们需要更改添加怪物的过程。 我们不希望将其添加为点击的一部分，而是希望点击发送消息。 将onClick函数的内容替换为以下内容：

 void OnMouseUp () { if ( CanPlaceMonster ()) { ablyManager . gameChannel . Publish ( "spot:" + name , “ 0 ” ); } else if ( CanUpgradeMonster ()) { MonsterData monsterData = monster . GetComponent < MonsterData >(); Debug . Log ( monsterData . levels . IndexOf ( monsterData . getNextLevel ())); ablyManager . gameChannel . Publish ( "spot:" + name , monsterData . levels . IndexOf ( monsterData . getNextLevel ()). ToString ()); } }

在这里，我们正在检查客户端是否可以实际执行我们正在尝试的操作，以避免发送不必要的消息。 假设我们可以，我们向 Ably 频道发送一条消息，其中包含与我们正在交互的地点相对应的名称字段，以及包含我们想要将怪物设置到的级别的数据字段。 名称字段很有用，因为 Ably 客户端库可以按名称过滤传入的消息，这将更容易在每个怪物的脚本中只处理针对每个怪物的消息。

订阅玩家输入

随着动作被发送到 Ably 频道，我们还需要让我们的客户订阅以接收这些消息。 为了在消息到达时保持消息，我们将使用队列。

这样做的主要原因是在消息进入时处理消息，因为 Ably 订阅功能将利用线程来主动侦听消息。 这意味着当收到一条消息时，该消息的处理将发生在这个新线程上而不是主线程上，这意味着我们将无法访问任何 Unity 的代码路径或其中的组件。 通过使用队列，我们​​可以处理稍后从更新函数从主线程接收到的消息。

让我们在类的顶部添加一个队列：

 private Queue actions = new Queue ();

现在我们有一个队列来保存我们的消息，让我们在 ablyManager 分配之后，在Start函数中添加一个订阅函数来监听 Ably 频道：

 ablyManager . gameChannel . Subscribe ( "spot:" + name , message => { // Need to uniquely identify actions to avoid unintentional upgrade when trying to place, etc. actions . Enqueue ( message . Data ); });

在这里，我们订阅了 Ably 频道，并且只对名称对应于“spot:”+名称的消息进行操作。 这意味着我们只会在队列中添加消息，如果它们是针对具有这个怪物名称的这个怪物的。

现在我们应该接收消息，我们需要对它们采取行动。 在Update函数中，我们可以开始遍历队列：

 // Update is called once per frame void Update () { if ( actions . Count == 0 ) return ; PlaceOrUpgradeMonster ( int . Parse (( string ) actions . Dequeue ())); }

在这里，我们正在检查队列是否有任何要执行的操作，如果是，我们将调用一个新函数PlaceOrUpgradeMonster ，传递为该位置请求的怪物级别。 这一点很重要，因为我们可能有多个客户端请求同时升级同一个图块，因此我们希望确保考虑到预期的升级级别，以避免无意中对图块进行进一步升级。

对于 PlaceOrUpgradeMonster 的内容，我们可以稍微调整OnMouseUp的旧内容，考虑到预期的升级级别以及是否仍然可能。

 private void PlaceOrUpgradeMonster ( int index ) { if ( CanPlaceMonster () && index == 0 ) { monster = ( GameObject ) Instantiate ( monsterPrefab , transform . position , Quaternion . identity ); AudioSource audioSource = gameObject . GetComponent < AudioSource >(); audioSource . PlayOneShot ( audioSource . clip ); gameManager . Gold -= monster . GetComponent < MonsterData >(). CurrentLevel . cost ; } else if ( CanUpgradeMonster ()) { MonsterData monsterData = monster . GetComponent < MonsterData >(); if ( monsterData . getNextLevel () == monsterData . levels [ index ]) { monster . GetComponent < MonsterData >(). increaseLevel (); AudioSource audioSource = gameObject . GetComponent < AudioSource >(); audioSource . PlayOneShot ( audioSource . clip ); gameManager . Gold -= monster . GetComponent < MonsterData >(). CurrentLevel . cost ; } } }

这是运行游戏并查看它是否有效的好时机！ 希望对于单个客户端，游戏应该和以前一样工作，点击瓷砖放置一个怪物。 如果您有两个客户端运行但使用相同的游戏 ID，则每个客户端的操作现在应该（希望）影响另一个客户端！

同步客户端

但是，我们仍然需要解决其他各种同步问题：

  • 我们如何确保所有客户端同时启动游戏
  • 我们如何处理延迟，这可能导致不同客户端在不同时间收到消息

同步开始时间

确保所有玩家同时开始是一件相当容易的事情； 我们可以在 UI 中添加一个按钮，玩家可以使用它来开始游戏。 这将向所有客户端发送一条消息，指示开始游戏。 通过 Ably 发送的每条消息也将附加一个时间戳，可用于确保所有客户端就游戏应该何时开始达成一致。

在游戏的 UI 中添加一个按钮，并将其命名为“StartButton”。 编辑附加到它的文本元素，使其具有文本“开始！”。

由于这将是方面游戏的流程，因此将以下内容添加到AblyManagerBehaviour脚本的顶部：

 public bool started = false ; public Button startButton ; public void StartGame () { startButton . enabled = false ; started = true ; }

在我们创建的按钮的 Inspector 中，在 Inspector 的On Click部分中选择AblyManager 。 从那里，转到函数下拉列表的AblyManagerBehaviour下拉列表，然后选择StartGame函数。 该按钮现在应该在点击时设置为paused为false 。

我们现在需要检查游戏是否已在 SpawnEnemy 脚本中启动。 首先，添加在SpawnEnemy脚本的Update函数内，将以下内容添加到顶部：

 if (! ablyManager . started ) { // This doesn't account for any time previously passed lastSpawnTime = Time . time ; return ; }

如果游戏还没有开始，我们只需将 lastSpawnTime 设置为当前时间，以避免敌人最终立即生成，然后跳过放置任何敌人的尝试。

我们还需要在 SpawnEnemy 中实例化SpawnEnemy ，所以在顶部添加以下行：

 private AblyManagerBehaviour ablyManager ;

接下来，替换Start的内容：

 void Start () { lastSpawnTime = Time . time ; gameManager = GameObject . Find ( "GameManager" ). GetComponent < GameManagerBehaviour >(); ablyManager = GameObject . Find ( "AblyManager" ). GetComponent < AblyManagerBehaviour >(); }

如果你现在运行游戏，本地游戏应该只在玩家点击开始按钮后开始。 然而，我们仍然需要与所有其他玩家分享这个开始动作。

为此，让我们在 AblyManagerBehaviour 类中创建一个函数，该函数将在单击按钮时发送消息，另一个函数将在客户端收到消息后调用 StartGame 函数：

 public void SendStartGame () { gameChannel . Publish ( "start" , "" ); }

在 AblyManagerBehaviour 的Awake函数中，我们可以添加一个订阅函数来监听这个消息，就像我们在PlaceMonster类中所做的那样：

 gameChannel . Subscribe ( "start" , ( msg ) => { StartGame (); });

如果您现在更改 StartButton 的 On Click Behavior 以使用这个新的SendStartGame功能，那么您现在应该希望拥有与之前在本地相同的 Behavior，但是所有客户端现在应该对任何客户端按下按钮做出反应。

确保保持同步

虽然上述指标很容易设置，但确保所有客户端在整个游戏中保持同步是一个更难的挑战。 您需要考虑几个基本场景：

  • 如果客户端滞后/冻结，导致其在游戏玩法方面落后怎么办？
  • 如果一条消息在到达某些客户端时出现延迟，会导致客户端之间出现不同的结果怎么办？

这两种情况都可能导致客户端之间的博弈结果不同，这是不可接受的。 有几种方法可以克服这些问题。

全状态通信

想象一下，您有一个中央服务器，它负责运行游戏的“真实”版本，并且作为所发生事情的事实真相而被信任。 每当它发生任何事情时，它都可以传达游戏的新状态，例如敌人在哪里，怪物在哪里，以及 cookie 和敌人的健康状况。

如果客户端落后了，它可以简单地检查最新版本的状态是什么，并调整游戏的本地表示以匹配。

客户端可能会在一小段时间内不同步，但当游戏的当前状态再次传达时，所有客户端都可以快速调整到当前状态并重新同步。 这意味着即使通信中存在延迟导致某些“同步”状态到达客户端不同步，它们也只会在一小段时间内不同步，但这不会影响它们在游戏中可以做的事情，也不所有客户端都会看到的最终结果，因为这是由服务器确定和传达的。

然而，与我们目前仅向实际与游戏交互的玩家发送消息的方法相比，这在通信方面相当繁重。

快进和延迟输入

为了确保相当可靠的同步状态，我们可以做的最简单的事情之一是结合两种技术。

首先，如果客户端由于他们的机器暂时冻结而在动作方面落后，它可以继续正常模拟游戏，直到它收到来自其他玩家的动作消息。 这些操作将附加一个时间戳，指示 Ably 何时收到消息。 如果我们将这些消息的时间戳与附加到初始“开始”消息的时间戳进行比较，我们可以看到从我们事实上的事实来源，即所有客户端共享的 Ably 服务器已经过去了多少时间。

如果我们跟踪自游戏开始以来调用 Update 函数的次数，我们可以解释本地游戏落后于游戏的“真实”状态多远。 如果我们然后快速循环游戏的滴答声直到我们回到“真实”状态，在适当的时间应用消息接收到的更新，我们应该让延迟的客户端重新同步。

对于某些用户来说，为了处理消息延迟到达的可能性，我们可以在绝大多数情况下通过简单地在游戏本身中引入延迟放置怪物并使其变得可操作（也就是能够开火）来处理这种情况。 如果我们将其设为 1 秒，那么怪物的放置消息在到达客户端时是否延迟最多 1 秒都没有关系，因为所有其他客户端都已经在等待附加到时间戳的 1 秒在允许怪物射击之前的消息，所以他们都会同时开始与敌人互动。

这并不完美，如果您想确保客户端可以保持同步的任何延迟，您需要实现某种形式的倒带机制来回放上一个“同步”时间点的操作，存储在本地这些时刻的游戏的确切状态，从在正确的时间执行“延迟”动作以确保它们在应该发生的时候发生。 不过，对于这个博客，我们将使用上述实现。

允许客户端赶上当前状态

让落后于当前状态的客户端赶上的最简单方法是使用 Unity 中的Time.timeScale变量，它会影响Update和FixedUpdate运行的频率。 当我们需要客户端赶上以快速运行事件直到客户端赶上当前状态，然后恢复正常速度时，我们可以扩大它。

但是，为了检测客户端是否落后，我们需要开始跟踪自本地游戏开始以来发生了多少滴答声，以及根据 Ably 的说法，游戏何时开始。 将以下内容添加到AblyManagerBehaviour类以开始跟踪自开始以来的滴答数：

 public int ticksSinceStart = 0 ; void FixedUpdate () { if ( started ) { ticksSinceStart ++; } }

添加后，我们还可以通过检查附加到所有客户端收到的“开始”消息的时间戳来根据 Ably 开始跟踪开始时间。 将 AblyManagerBehaviour 中的StartGame和Awake函数AblyManagerBehaviour为以下内容：

 public DateTimeOffset ? startTimeAbly ; // Start is called before the first frame update void Awake () { gameChannel = realtime . Channels . Get ( StateManagerBehaviour . Instance . GameID ); gameChannel . Subscribe ( "start" , ( msg ) => { StartGame ( msg . Timestamp ); }); } public void StartGame ( DateTimeOffset ? timestamp ) { if (! started ) startTimeAbly = timestamp ; started = true ; }

有了这个startTimeAbly应该包含游戏开始的时间戳。

现在从游戏开始开始跟踪经过的滴答声，除了根据 Ably 游戏开始的时间，我们需要开始使用从 Ably 到客户端的消息的时间戳来确定我们当前的游戏是否状态对于经过的时间是正确的。

我们需要做的第一件事是开始将来自 Ably 的消息的时间戳传递到PlaceMonster类到我们消费的队列。 让我们在PlaceMonster文件中创建一个对象，以表示level of monster to upgrade to时间戳：

 public class PlaceMonsterData { public int monsterID ; public DateTimeOffset ? timestamp ; }

让我们替换PlaceMonster中Start函数中的subscribe调用以使用新结构：

 ablyManager . gameChannel . Subscribe ( "spot:" + name , message => { PlaceMonsterData pmd = new PlaceMonsterData (); pmd . monsterID = int . Parse (( string ) message . Data ); pmd . timestamp = message . Timestamp ; actions . Enqueue ( pmd ); });

接下来，更改Update函数以使用新的数据结构：

 void Update () { if ( actions . Count == 0 ) return ; PlaceMonsterData pmd = ( PlaceMonsterData ) actions . Peek (); DateTimeOffset ? startTime = ablyManager . startTimeAbly ; DateTimeOffset ? msgTime = pmd . timestamp ; TimeSpan ? diffTime = msgTime - startTime ; int ticksSince = ablyManager . ticksSinceStart ; float timeFromTicks = ticksSince * ( 1000 * Time . fixedDeltaTime ); if (! diffTime . HasValue ) { PlaceOrUpgradeMonster ( pmd . monsterID ); return ; } if ( timeFromTicks < diffTime . Value . TotalMilliseconds ) { Time . timeScale = 20 ; return ; } else { Time . timeScale = 1 ; actions . Dequeue (); PlaceOrUpgradeMonster ( pmd . monsterID ); } }

让我们分解一下我们在这里做什么。 我们将从AblyManagerBehaviour类中检查开始时间和当前刻度，并将其与队列中的当前下一个元素进行比较。 我们使用Peek检查下一个元素，因为如果我们在当前更新循环中不同步，我们可能不想使用它。

我们通过比较开始时间戳和动作的时间戳并将其存储在diffTime中来计算自游戏开始以来经过的时间量。

然后，我们可以通过计算已经发生的滴答数，并将其乘以每个滴答应发生的时间量（默认为每 20 毫秒一次）来计算自游戏开始以来已经过去了多少有效time 。 该值保存在Time.fixedDeltaTime中，因此我们可以使用它来计算timeFromTicks 。

然后我们可以比较实际经过的时间 (diffTime) 和本地游戏经过的时间 (timeFromTicks)。 如果游戏时间落后，我们可以使用Time.timeScale加快游戏速度。 在这里它设置为 20，但您可以随意调整它以匹配您感觉最好的任何东西。

如果我们达到游戏赶上真正“状态”的时间，我们最终可以通过放置怪物来执行消息，并确保 timeScale 返回到 1。

您现在应该能够通过运行游戏来测试这一点。 从大厅场景开始，输入房间代码，然后点击开始按钮。 你应该会发现一开始敌人应该以正常的速度移动，怪物的放置和往常一样。

但是，如果您执行了导致游戏暂停的操作，例如将任务分配给另一个应用程序，则当您返回时，您应该会看到游戏正常进行，直到您尝试放置一个新怪物。 然后游戏应该加快步伐以赶上游戏应该处于的真实状态！

这有助于确保我们最终与主状态保持一致，但取决于发生的新动作以使客户端回到真实的当前状态。 由于当前调用动作的唯一方法是放置怪物，这种同步可能会立即发生，或者在游戏结束前根本不会发生。 一个简单的解决方法是从客户端引入一个相当规则的 ping，以确保所有客户端始终从 Ably 接收时间戳以进行同步。

延迟怪物放置

我们已经处理了一个客户端落后的情况，但我们还没有解决消息被延迟的问题，因此当一个客户端出现时说怪物不存在，而另一个客户端却不存在，导致对敌人造成的伤害不同步。

理想情况下，我们有一个万无一失的方法来处理这种情况，例如通过在本地使用游戏状态中的检查点来回退到可能导致不同步的时刻，例如放置怪物，然后从该点快速重播游戏向前。

然而，在这个演示中，我们将做一些更简单的事情，但它适用于绝大多数场景； 从动作发生时延迟怪物的放置。 也就是说，如果我们收到一条消息要放置一个时间戳为“1000”的怪物，我们不会在本地游戏中说“2000”之前将炮塔放置在游戏中。 这意味着，只要所有客户端都在消息真正出现和其中任何一个实际执行该消息之间的 1 秒内收到此消息，他们就应该在游戏中完全相同的时间执行该操作。

为此，我们可以调整拍摄逻辑，使其在与升级/放置相关联的时间戳后一秒后才拍摄。 从玩家的角度来看，这应该使怪物的实际放置仍然感觉流畅和即时，实际的“延迟”动作被怪物射击的延迟所掩盖。

首先，我们需要将时间戳添加到ShootEnemies类中。 将以下变量添加到类的顶部：

 private AblyManagerBehaviour ablyManager ; public DateTimeOffset ? Timestamp ;

接下来，在Start函数中添加对 AblyManagerBehaviour 的引用：

 ablyManager = gameObject . GetComponentInChildren < AblyManagerBehaviour >();

最后，在Update函数的开头添加对时间戳的考虑：

 if ( timestamp . HasValue ) { DateTimeOffset ? startTime = ablyManager . startTimeAbly ; DateTimeOffset ? msgTime = timestamp . GetValueOrDefault (); TimeSpan ? diffTime = msgTime - startTime ; int ticksSince = ablyManager . ticksSinceStart ; float timeFromTicks = ticksSince * ( 1000 * Time . fixedDeltaTime ); if ( timeFromTicks >= diffTime . Value . TotalMilliseconds + 1000 ) { timestamp = null ; } else { return ; } }

添加后，我们需要一种将时间戳传递给怪物的ShootEnemies类的方法。 将PlaceMonster中的PlaceOrUpgradeMonster函数更新为以下内容：

 private void PlaceOrUpgradeMonster ( int monsterLevel , DateTimeOffset ? timestamp ) { if ( CanPlaceMonster () && monsterLevel == 0 ) { monster = ( GameObject ) Instantiate ( monsterPrefab , transform . position , Quaternion . identity ); monster . GetComponent < ShootEnemies >(). timestamp = timestamp ; AudioSource audioSource = gameObject . GetComponent < AudioSource >(); audioSource . PlayOneShot ( audioSource . clip ); gameManager . Gold -= monster . GetComponent < MonsterData >(). CurrentLevel . cost ; } else if ( CanUpgradeMonster ()) { MonsterData monsterData = monster . GetComponent < MonsterData >(); if ( monsterData . getNextLevel () == monsterData . levels [ monsterLevel ]) { monster . GetComponent < MonsterData >(). increaseLevel (); monster . GetComponent < ShootEnemies >(). timestamp = timestamp ; AudioSource audioSource = gameObject . GetComponent < AudioSource >(); audioSource . PlayOneShot ( audioSource . clip ); gameManager . Gold -= monster . GetComponent < MonsterData >(). CurrentLevel . cost ; } } }

有了这个，我们允许从Update函数传递时间戳，然后我们使用它在ShootEnemies类中分配timestamp值。

最后，更改PlaceMonster类中的Update函数以将时间戳传递给PlaceOrUpgradeMonster函数：

 … if (! diffTime . HasValue ) { PlaceOrUpgradeMonster ( pmd . monsterID , msgTime ); return ; } if ( timeFromTicks < diffTime . Value . TotalMilliseconds ) { Time . timeScale = 20 ; return ; } else { Time . timeScale = 1 ; actions . Dequeue (); PlaceOrUpgradeMonster ( pmd . monsterID , msgTime ); }

With that, we should now have decent handling for clients both falling behind the current state, in addition to clients receiving delayed messages.

结论

We have a multiplayer Tower Defence game which can handle network and client instability. There's a load of additional features, improvements, and fixes that can still be applied at this stage, but it's a strong starting point to take further and experiment with. A few things I'd recommend trying out are:

  • Making use of Token Authentication for clients, to keep authentication details away from untrusted users
  • Having a central server to further coordinate users, and perform additional validation, provide scoreboards, and more
  • Extending the synchronization work to support rewinding the state of a game to a previously known state
  • Giving clients names so players can uniquely identify themselves
  • Providing players their own money to buy monsters with rather than a shared bank

There is a version of the game available from the browser if you'd like to give it a try.

If you create anything based on this work, I'd love to hear about it in the comments, on Twitter , or even directly via email .



原文:

Some of the funnest games are those you can play with your friends. Being able to explore worlds, defeat monsters, and overcome puzzles and challenges together can be amazing experiences.

Creating multiplayer functionality in games can be a massive challenge however. Creating architectures that allow for reliable communication of clients, cheat detection, handling of networking issues and more requires a lot of thought and planning.

In this blog, we'll be exploring a lot of the considerations required in multiplayer gaming and demonstrating some of the best ways to make things work smoothly and easily.

Introduction

In order to best demonstrate multiplayer functionality in Unity, rather than creating an entire game from the ground up, I got in touch with raywenderlich to see if I could extend their Tower Defense tutorial series with multiplayer functionality. It’s an incredibly detailed and informative tutorial of a tower defense game, and I strongly recommend checking it out to fully understand the context of this blog post.

With this core, single-player game, we have a solid basis for adding in multiplayer functionality. Core features and considerations we'll cover in this blog are:

  • How to communicate placement and upgrading of turrets
  • How to keep clients in sync with an authoritative source
  • How to handle clients falling out of sync with one another, be it due to latencies, computer issues, and more
  • How to handle re-connection of clients to a game
