item.title: 什么是 SAST - 静态应用程序安全测试？ | What is SAST - Static Application Security Testing?
item.description:
安全威胁和数据泄露变得越来越普遍，可能会对您的组织产生巨大的财务和业务影响。 因此，您必须能够快速检测应用程序中的漏洞。

有几种方法可以找到安全漏洞，我们将详细介绍两种应用程序安全测试方法：

在本文中，我们将重点关注静态应用程序安全测试 (SAST) 。 继续阅读以了解 SAST 是什么、为什么它很重要以及它的优缺点。

什么是 SAST？

静态应用程序安全测试 (SAST) 是一种白盒测试方法，它检查源代码以发现软件漏洞、缺陷和弱点。 这些漏洞包括 SQL 注入攻击、跨站点脚本、缓冲区溢出以及 OWASP 十大安全风险中列出的其他漏洞。

您的团队应该在整个代码库的开发生命周期中尽早并经常执行 SAST。 在开发生命周期的早期发现这些漏洞使您的开发人员的生活更容易交付高质量的代码并增强整体应用程序的安全性。

为什么 SAST 很重要？

通过这样做，SAST 工具还降低了解决问题的成本及其潜在影响。 例如，如果您在部署应用程序之前没有发现漏洞，则可能导致严重的数据泄露，从而给您的组织带来经济损失和损害。

因此，将 SAST 集成到您的软件开发生命周期中可以显着提高代码的整体质量。

一些 SAST 工具会指出漏洞的位置并突出显示有问题的代码，指导您如何解决问题。 因此，开发人员可以获得有关其代码质量的实时反馈，这有助于他们从以前的错误中吸取教训，并在未来开发出更安全的代码。

但是，它们无法识别运行时发生的漏洞。 与每种方法一样，SAST 也有其优点和缺点。 让我们仔细看看它们。

不需要执行源代码：SAST 在应用程序运行之前对源代码进行处理。 因此，SAST 扫描可以非常快。
  • 修复漏洞更便宜：由于 SAST 不需要部署的应用程序，因此可以在开发生命周期的早期执行，从而减少修复已识别漏洞所需的成本和时间。
  • 显示有问题的代码位置： SAST 可以将您指向漏洞的确切位置，让您可以立即修复问题。 因此，您和您的团队无需花费数天时间来挖掘源代码，从而减少了开发时间。
  • 不需要测试用例： SAST 工具会将其整个规则集应用于您的代码库。 通过这样做，它可以让您捕捉到您可能会错过的漏洞。
  • 轻松自动化和快速扫描： SAST不需要很多配置，使自动化变得简单易行。
  • 扫描速度比人类快。 事实上，有些工具可以在短短几分钟内扫描数千行代码。 因此，当您将 SAST 工具集成到您的开发生命周期中时，您的团队可以专注于编码而不是安全检查。
  • 检测常见漏洞： SAST 工具可以识别与常见漏洞相关的代码模式，例如 OWASP 十大安全风险。
  • 兼容多种编程语言。 因此，您应该可以轻松找到适合您团队需求的 SAST 工具。

    • 报告误报的高风险： SAST 适用于源代码，因此它通常不会考虑整个情况。 有些问题是误报，这意味着它们可能不是问题。 因此，开发人员会大量分析 SAST 工具的结果，以确定它们是否代表真正的安全风险，这可能会减慢开发速度。
  • 编程语言依赖性： SAST 分析您的源代码，这意味着它是特定于语言的。 对于大多数主流编程语言（例如，Java 和 C），有很多可用的 SAST 工具，但对于小众语言（例如，ReScript 和 Clojure），只有很少的 SAST 工具。
  • 报告很快就会过时：由于 SAST 工具只生成静态报告，因此这些报告很快就会过时。 因此，您必须在开发生命周期中多次运行 SAST 扫描以捕获新的代码错误或安全漏洞。
  • 不分析运行漏洞： SAST 工具无法识别动态环境中的漏洞，无法有效发现配置错误和运行时问题。
  • 困难的初始设置：如果您已经拥有大型代码库，SAST 的初始设置并不容易。 因此，您需要选择一种在设置时挑战较少的工具。
  • 需要获得多个 SAST 工具：有时，建议拥有多个 SAST 工具，因为一个可能无法检测到应用程序中的所有漏洞。

    • 我们很自豪能够在 SAST 工具可用性和实施​​指数中排名第一。 这是因为 Codacy 是业内最容易实施和开始使用的工具之一。

    如果您正在寻找一种允许您检查代码质量并跟踪您的技术债务的静态分析工具，请立即试用 Codacy 。

    切尔西德弗罗 - 一旦暂停，codacy 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被取消。

    一旦解除暂停，codacy 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦未发布，所有由 codacy 发布的帖子都将被隐藏，并且只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 codacy 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。



    原文:

    Security threats and data breaches have become more common and may have huge financial and business implications for your organization. As such, you must be able to detect vulnerabilities in your applications fast.

    There are several ways to find security vulnerabilities, and we’ll cover two application security testing methods in detail:

    In this article, we are focusing on Static Application Security Testing (SAST). Keep reading to know what SAST is, why it is important, and its pros and cons.

    What is SAST?

    Static application security testing (SAST) is a white-box testing method that examines the source code to find software vulnerabilities, flaws, and weaknesses. These vulnerabilities include SQL injection attacks, cross-site scripting, buffer overflows, and others listed in the OWASP Top 10 security risks. 

    Your team should perform SAST early and often in the development lifecycle against the entire codebase. SAST tools scan the application from the inside out to identify vulnerabilities in the code before compilation or execution. Finding these vulnerabilities earlier in the development lifecycle makes the life of your developers easier to ship high-quality code and enhances the overall application security.

    Why is SAST important?

    ​​SAST tools automatically identify critical vulnerabilities earlier in the software development lifecycle, enabling you to shift security left. By doing so, SAST tools also decrease the cost of fixing the issues and their potential impact. For example, if you didn’t find vulnerabilities before you deploy the application, it could lead to a severe data breach, resulting in financial loss and damage to your organization.

    SAST tools analyze your entire codebase, and they are much faster than manual code reviews performed by humans, scanning thousands of lines of code in a matter of minutes. As a result, integrating SAST into your software development lifecycle can significantly improve the overall quality of your code.

    Some SAST tools point out the vulnerabilities' location and highlight problematic code, guiding you on how to fix the issues. As such, developers have real-time feedback on the quality of their code, which helps them learn from previous mistakes and develop more secure code in the future.

    SAST can also help you in the process of being compliant with standards or getting certifications, like ISO/IEC 27001:2013 or PCI DSS.

    SAST pros & cons

    SAST tools are great for analyzing source code. However, they cannot identify vulnerabilities happening during runtime. As in every method, SAST has its pros and cons. Let’s take a close look at them.

    Pros of SAST

    • Does not require source code execution: SAST works on the source code before the application runs. As such, SAST scans can be incredibly fast.
    • Fixing vulnerabilities is cheaper: Since SAST doesn’t need a deployed application, it can be performed earlier in the development lifecycle, reducing the cost and time required to fix the identified vulnerabilities.
    • Shows problematic code locations: SAST can point you to the exact location of a vulnerability, allowing you to fix the issues immediately. As a result, you and your team don’t spend days digging through source code, and you cut down on development time.
    • Does not require test cases: SAST tools will apply their entire rule set to your codebase. By doing so, it allows you to catch vulnerabilities you might otherwise miss.
    • Easy automation and fast scanning: SAST does not need many configurations, making the automation simple and easy.
    • Faster at scanning than humans. SAST analyzes your entire codebase faster than humans. In fact, some tools can scan thousands of code lines in just a few minutes. So when you integrate a SAST tool into your development lifecycle, your team can focus on coding instead of security checking.
    • Detection of common vulnerabilities: SAST tools can identify code patterns associated with common vulnerabilities, such as OWASP Top 10 security risks.
    • Compatible with several programming languages. SAST tools are available for most mainstream languages and frameworks. As such, you shouldn't have trouble finding an appropriate SAST tool for your team’s needs.

    Cons of SAST

    • High risk of reporting false positives:  SAST works on source code, so it often doesn’t consider the entire picture. Some issues are false positives, meaning they may not be a problem. As such, developers much analyze the SAST tool results to determine whether they represent real security risks, which can slow down development.
    • Programming language dependency: SAST analyzes your source code, meaning it is language-specific. For most mainstream programming languages (e.g., Java and C), there are a lot of SAST tools available, but for niche languages (e.g., ReScript and Clojure), there are very few SAST tools.
    • Reports become outdated quickly: Since SAST tools only generate static reports, those reports become obsolete quickly. As such, you must run a SAST scan multiple times during the development lifecycle to catch new code errors or security vulnerabilities.
    • No analysis of running vulnerabilities: SAST tools cannot identify vulnerabilities in dynamic environments, making them ineffective in finding configuration errors and runtime issues.
    • Difficult initial setup: The initial setup of SAST, if you already have a large codebase, does not come easy. SAST tools usually detect many issues when the tools check source code for the first time. So you need to choose a tool that will give you fewer challenges when setting it up.
    • Need to get more than one SAST tool: Sometimes, it is advisable to have more than one SAST tool as one may not detect all the vulnerabilities in an application.

    G2 nominates Codacy as the Easiest to Implement and Use SAST tool

    We are proud to rank 1st in the SAST tools Usability and Implementation indexes. This is due to the fact that Codacy is one of the easiest to implement and start using tools in the industry.

    If you’re looking for a static analysis tool that allows you to check your code quality and keep track of your technical debt, try out Codacy today.

    Get started for free

    <!-- /wp:button -->

    Chelsea Devereaux - Jun 28

    Once suspended, codacy will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

    Once unsuspended, codacy will be able to comment and publish posts again.

    Once unpublished, all posts by codacy will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

    If codacy is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

item.title: F# 使用选项将类型 A 的列表转换为类型 B | F# Convert a List of Type A to Type B with options
item.description:
罗伯·穆尔彼得

发表于本文的目的是展示如何在 F# 中将一种类型的列表转换为另一种类型。 虽然在标题中使用了“转换”，但这实际上是在使用“地图”功能并返回一个全新的列表。

下面的代码是在.NET 6的console application中编写的

type Person = { name : string } type Employee = { email : string } let ConvertPersonToEmployee ( person : Person ) : Employee = { email = person . name + "@fsharp.com" } let ConvertPersonListToEmployeeList ( people : List < Person > option ) : List < Employee > option = match people with | None -> None | _ -> people . Value |> List . map ConvertPersonToEmployee |> Some [< EntryPoint >] let main argv = let januaryJoiners : List < Person > option = Some [{ name = "Rob" }; { name = "Bob" }] let februaryJoiners : List < Person > option = None let januaryEmployees = ConvertPersonListToEmployeeList januaryJoiners let februaryEmployees = ConvertPersonListToEmployeeList februaryJoiners printfn $"January Employees: { januaryEmployees } " printfn $"February Employees: { februaryEmployees } " 0

输出

January Employees : Some ([{ email = "Rob@fsharp.com" }; { email = "Bob@fsharp.com" }]) February Employees :

一旦暂停，robmulpeter 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，robmulpeter 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，robmulpeter 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 robmulpeter 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。



原文:

Rob Mulpeter

Posted on Jul 21

The purpose of this article is a small demonstration of how one can convert a list of one type to another type in F#. While 'Convert' is used in the header, this is really using the 'map' function and returning an entirely new list.

The below code was written in a console application in .NET 6

type Person = { name: string } type Employee = { email: string
} let ConvertPersonToEmployee (person: Person) : Employee = { email = person.name + "@fsharp.com" } let ConvertPersonListToEmployeeList (people: List<Person> option) : List<Employee> option = match people with | None -> None | _ -> people.Value |> List.map ConvertPersonToEmployee |> Some [<EntryPoint>]
let main argv = let januaryJoiners : List<Person> option = Some [{ name = "Rob" }; { name = "Bob" }] let februaryJoiners : List<Person> option = None let januaryEmployees = ConvertPersonListToEmployeeList januaryJoiners let februaryEmployees = ConvertPersonListToEmployeeList februaryJoiners printfn $"January Employees: {januaryEmployees}" printfn $"February Employees: {februaryEmployees}" 0

Output

January Employees: Some([{ email = "Rob@fsharp.com" }; { email = "Bob@fsharp.com" }])
February Employees:

Once unsuspended, robmulpeter will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by robmulpeter will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If robmulpeter is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

item.title: 让我们讨论提交消息 | Let's discuss commit messages
item.description:
我们都知道什么是提交消息，并且可能每天至少写 1 条提交消息。 但是，我们有多大信心在一段时间后通过仅阅读消息来理解我们在提交中所做的更改？

我们没有编写正确的提交消息有多种原因：

  • 团队/项目中没有定义适当的结构
  • 懒惰

也可能有各种各样的其他原因。

可是等等！

为什么我们应该编写更好的提交消息？

如果我们浏览我们任何存储库的git log ，很有可能它看起来像这样，

 fixed NPE in test case CSS fixes added a button to send mail updates versions in package.json

嗯，那么最新提交中的修复是什么？ 是的，即使我在查看提交中的更改之前也无法判断。 也许您在专业环境中遇到过代码，您不知道它在做什么或意味着什么。

与拥有可维护的代码和良好的文档类似，建议拥有良好的提交日志以节省您和/或同事在存储库中挖掘的时间。

提交消息的剖析

我们大多数人只写一行提交消息，通过运行以下命令来解释更改是什么。

 git commit -m "<Message>"

但是你知道多行提交是可能的吗？

 git commit -m "<Message1>" -m "<Message2>"

实际上，对于git commit命令的每个-m参数，它都会在提交消息中添加一个新行。

此功能为我们编写提交打开了一个全新的世界，因为我们现在知道我们不会因为在 1 行中写下我们的更改而受到阻碍，我们可以在一行中进行摘要，在后续行中进行详细说明。 承诺的结构将如下

git commit -m "<Title>" -m "<Message>" -m "<Footer>"

专业提示：在编写提交消息而不是命令行时更喜欢使用文本编辑器：D

让我们谈谈更好的提交消息

由于我们现在已经定义了提交消息的基本结构，让我们在结构中添加一些规则，以在我们所有的提交中保持一致性。

这些规则对我有用，但可能对你不起作用。 我的目标是给你一些灵感，这样你就可以想出你的结构。

提交类型

等什么！！！ 有哪些类型的提交？

从技术上讲，没有类型，但从逻辑上讲，我们可以根据需要拥有任意数量的类型。 以下是一些被广泛接受的提交类型：

  • 与修复或功能无关且不修改 src 或测试文件的chore更改（例如更新依赖项）
  • 为了指定提交的类型，我使用 emoji 和 🐛 来分别表示专长和 bug……更多不同类型提交的 emoji 可以在这里找到。 将类型指定为文本也可以，但我已经转换为这种编写提交的方式，原因有两个：

    1. 我在主题行中保存了一些字符

    提交范围

    范围是一个名词，描述了代码库的哪个部分受此更改/提交的影响。

    例子：

     - feat(API) or (API) - fix(orders) or 🐛(orders) - docs(workflow) or 📝(workflow)

    主题

    提交中更改的摘要。

    编写主题时需要考虑的一些关键点：

    • 有 50 个字符的硬性限制
    • 无需添加标点符号，请注意我们设置的硬限制；）
    • 使用祈使语气

    身体

    这是一个可选部分，我们可以在其中详细说明更改内容以及我们进行更改的原因。

    • 用空行将主体与主体分开
    • 尝试在身体的每一行都有一个硬限制（我更喜欢有 70 到 75 左右）

    页脚

    此部分可用作提交的元部分。 在这里，我们可以指定更改对应的票证或问题编号等内容。 提交的审阅者等，

    这些信息的大部分灵感来自常规提交，它们详细解释了编写良好提交的所有规范。 请注意，没有一种正确的提交提交方式，但拥有团队协议可以帮助更轻松地维护存储库。

    工具

    如果您喜欢编写常规提交的概念并希望将它们强制纳入您的工作流程，那么有很多工具可以帮助您做到这一点。

    • 如果您更喜欢使用命令行来编写提交，那么 Commitizen对您来说是一个不错的选择，当您即将提交您的更改时，它会询问一系列问题并强制您编写描述性提交。
    • 如果您更喜欢使用 IntelliJ 或 VS Code 等 IDE，则可以使用插件。 我在 IntelliJ 中使用过这个插件，它对我有用，但是，我没有在 VSCode 中尝试过。

    参考



原文:

We all know what a commit message is, and probably write at least 1 commit message every day. But how confident we are that we'll understand the changes we made in a commit by reading only the message after some time?

There are multiple reasons why we don't write proper commit messages:

  • No proper structure is defined in the team/project
  • Laziness

There can be a wide variety of other reasons as well.

But wait!

Why should we write better commit messages?

If we browse through the git log of any of our repositories, there is a high chance that it looks something like this,

fixed NPE in test case
CSS fixes
added a button to send mail
updates versions in package.json

Hmm, so what is the fix in the latest commit? Yeah, even I can't tell until I look at the changes in the commit. Perhaps you have encountered code in a professional environment where you had no idea what it was doing or meant for.

Similar to having maintainable code and good documentation, having a good commit log is suggested to save yourself and/or co-workers hours of digging around in the repository.

Anatomy of a commit message

Most of us write only one line of commit message explaining what the changes are by running the following command.

git commit -m "<Message>"

But did you know that multi-line commits are possible?

git commit -m "<Message1>" -m "<Message2>"

Actually, for each -m param to the git commit command it adds a new line to the commit message.

This feature opens a whole new world for us to write commits, as we now know that we aren't blocked by writing about our changes in 1 line, we can have a summary in one line have a detailed explanation in the subsequent lines. The structure of the commitment will come as

git commit -m "<Title>" -m "<Message>" -m "<Footer>"

Pro Tip: Prefer using a text editor when writing commit messages instead of command-line :D

Let's talk about better commit messages

As we now have defined a basic structure of the commit message, let's add some rules along with the structure to have consistency across all of our commits.

These rules work for me, but might not work for you. My goal here is to give you some inspiration so that you can come up with your structure.

Type of Commit

Wait what!!! There are types of commits?

Technically there are no types, but logically we can have any number of types based on our needs. Following are a few of the widely accepted types of commits:

  • feat Added a new feature
  • fix Fixed a bug
  • chore Changes that do not relate to a fix or feature and don't modify src or test files (for example updating dependencies)
  • refactor refactored code that neither fixes a bug nor adds a feature
  • docs Updates to docs in the repository
  • style Format code
  • test Adding/fixing test cases
  • perf Performance improvements
  • ci Continuous Integration related
  • build Changes that affect the build system or external dependencies
  • revert Reverts a previous commit

To specify the type of the commit I use emojis like , and 🐛 for the feat, and bug respectively... more emojis for different types of commits can be found here. Specifying the type as text would also work, but I've transitioned to this way of writing commits for 2 reasons:

  1. Git log looks cool 😎
  2. I save some characters in the subject line

Scope of Commit

The scope is a noun that describes what section of the codebase is affected by this change/commit.

Example:

- feat(API) or (API)
- fix(orders) or 🐛(orders)
- docs(workflow) or 📝(workflow)

Subject

Summary of the changes in the commit.

Some key points to consider while writing the subject:

  • Have a hard limit of 50 characters
  • There is no need to add punctuation, be mindful of the hard limit we've set ;)
  • Use the imperative mood

Body

This is an optional section where we can provide a detailed explanation of what the change is, and why we've made the change.

  • Separate the subject from the body with a blank line
  • Try having a hard limit in each line of the body(I prefer to have around 70 to 75)

Footer

This section can be used as a meta section of the commit. Here we can specify things like the ticket or issue number that the change corresponds to. reviewer of the commit etc..,

Most of this information is inspired from Conventional Commits and they explain all the specs for writing a good commit in much detail. Do note that there is no one correct way of writing commits but having a team agreement can help maintain the repository easier.

Tools

If you've liked the concept of writing conventional commits and want to force them into your workflow, there are a lot of tools available to help you to do so.

  • If you prefer using the command line for writing commits, Commitizen would be a good choice for you, when you are about to commit your changes it asks a series of questions and forces you to write descriptive commits.
  • If you prefer using IDEs like IntelliJ or VS Code there are plugins available. I've used this plugin in IntelliJ and it worked for me, however, I haven't tried any in VSCode.

References
item.author: memanikanta

item.title: 卡恩算法的拓扑排序 | Topological Sort with Kahn's Algorithm
item.description:
里奥·菲佛

发表于在最近的一个项目中，我们正在开发一个类似电子表格的工具，用户可以在其中交叉引用公式中的不同行，就像在 Excel 中一样。 我们遇到了如何对行的评估进行排序以使依赖关系不会引起任何冲突的问题。

例如，假设我们有三行：

行号公式渲染值
row1 manual input 第 3 row3 * 2 第 1 row1 + 1 但是， row2引用row3 。 因此，为了渲染row2 ，我们首先需要渲染row3 。

事实证明，这是一个很常见的问题，例如在解决项目中不同模块的导入顺序时。

我们可以使用拓扑排序来解决这些类型的问题，它采用 DAG（有向无环图）并对其节点进行排序，使得对于每条边x -> y ， x在排序中位于y之前。

卡恩算法是一种可以建立这种拓扑顺序的算法。

卡恩算法：理论

卡恩算法背后的想法很简单：

  1. 将所有没有依赖关系的节点按顺序排列
  2. 从所有剩余节点的依赖关系中删除所有这些节点的出现。 如果没有循环，这将为您提供没有依赖关系的新节点。
  3. 重复直到没有更多的节点。

例如：

节点取决于
a 这是依赖关系图（其中x -> y表示x依赖于y 。

因此：

  1. 把它按顺序排列。
    订单 = [ d ]

  2. 从其他依赖项中删除出现的d 。 因此，在从这些依赖项中删除d之后， b和c都没有任何依赖项。

  1. 然后按顺序排列。
    顺序 = [ d , b , c ]

  2. 从其他依赖项中删除b和c的出现。 因此，在从这些依赖项中删除b和c之后， a不再具有依赖项。

  1. 把它按顺序排列。 顺序 = [ d , b , c , a ]

  1. 没有更多的节点了。 完毕。 最终订单 = [ d , b , c , a ]

卡恩算法：实现

对于我们的实现，我们假设输入一个字典，其中键是节点名称，值是依赖于键的所有节点的列表。

例如，如果节点B和C依赖于A ，那么字典中的条目将是A: [B, C] 。

将上一节的理论翻译成 Python 代码，可能的实现如下所示：

 def topological_sort ( nodes : dict [ str , list [ str ]]) -> list [ str ]: """ Topological sort for a network of nodes nodes = {"A": ["B", "C"], "B": [], "C": ["B"]} topological_sort(nodes) # ["A", "C", "B"] :param nodes: Nodes of the network :return: nodes in topological order """ # Calculate the indegree for each node indegrees = { k : 0 for k in nodes . keys ()} for name , dependencies in nodes . items (): for dependency in dependencies : indegrees [ dependency ] += 1 # Place all elements with indegree 0 in queue queue = [ k for k in nodes . keys () if indegrees [ k ] == 0 ] final_order = [] # Continue until all nodes have been dealt with while len ( queue ) > 0 : # node of current iteration is the first one from the queue curr = queue . pop ( 0 ) final_order . append ( curr ) # remove the current node from other dependencies for dependency in nodes [ curr ]: indegrees [ dependency ] -= 1 if indegrees [ dependency ] == 0 : queue . append ( dependency ) # check for circular dependencies if len ( final_order ) != len ( nodes ): raise Exception ( "Circular dependency found." ) return final_order

让我们用一个稍微进化的网络来试试这个：

相应的输入如下所示：

 nodes = { "A" : [ "B" , "C" ], "B" : [ "C" , "D" ], "C" : [ "F" ], "D" : [ "F" ], "E" : [ "F" ], "F" : [] }

如果我们运行它，我们会得到以下顺序：

 order = topological_sort ( nodes ) print ( order ) # ['A', 'E', 'B', 'C', 'D', 'F']

再看一下图表，让自己相信这确实是一个正确的顺序，可以避免依赖冲突！

菲尔·坦特罗马诺—— 一旦暂停，leopfeiffer 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，leopfeiffer 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，leopfeiffer 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 leopfeiffer 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。



原文:

Leo Pfeiffer

Posted on Jul 21

On a recent project, we were working on a spreadsheet-like tool where users could cross-reference different rows in formula, just like in Excel. We ran into the question of how to order the evaluation of the rows such that the dependencies do not cause any conflict.

For example, imagine we have three rows:

Row id Formula Rendered values
row1 manual input 1, 2, 3
row2 row3 * 2 6, 8, 6
row3 row1 + 1 2, 3, 4

row1 can be rendered directly, as its formula does not depend on any other rows. row2 however, references row3. row3 references row1. Thus in order to render row2, we first need to render row3.

It turns out, this is quite a common problem, for example in resolving the import order of different modules in a project.

We can solve these types of problems using topological sorting, which takes a DAG (directed acyclical graph) and orders its nodes in a way that for every edge x -> y, x comes before y in the ordering.

Kahn's algorithm is one algorithm that can establish this topological order.

Kahn's Algorithm: Theory

The idea behind Kahn's algorithm is simple:

  1. Put all nodes that have no dependencies in your order
  2. Remove all occurrences of those nodes from the dependencies of all remaining nodes. If there are no cycles, this will give you new nodes without dependencies.
  3. Repeat until there are no more nodes left.

For example:

Node Depends on
a b, c
b d
c d
d -

Here's the dependency graph (where x -> y means that x depends on y.

Thus:

  1. d has no dependencies. Put it in the order.
    Order = [d]

  2. Remove occurrences of d from the other dependencies. d occurs in the dependencies of b, c. Thus, after removing d from those dependencies, neither b nor c have any dependencies.

  1. b and c have no dependencies. Put then in the order.
    Order = [d, b, c]

  2. Remove occurrences of b and c from the other dependencies. Both b and c occur in the dependencies of a. Thus, after removing b and c from those dependencies, a has no more dependencies.

  1. a has no dependencies. Put it in the order. Order = [d, b, c, a]

  1. No more nodes left. Done. Final order = [d, b, c, a]

Kahn's Algorithm: Implementation

For our implementation we assume as input a dictionary where the keys are the node names and the values are a list of all nodes that the depend on the key.

For example, if nodes B and C depend on A, then on entry in the dictionary would be A: [B, C].

Translating the theory from the previous section into Python code, a possible implementation looks like this:

def topological_sort(nodes: dict[str, list[str]]) -> list[str]: """ Topological sort for a network of nodes nodes = {"A": ["B", "C"], "B": [], "C": ["B"]} topological_sort(nodes) # ["A", "C", "B"] :param nodes: Nodes of the network :return: nodes in topological order """ # Calculate the indegree for each node
 indegrees = {k: 0 for k in nodes.keys()} for name, dependencies in nodes.items(): for dependency in dependencies: indegrees[dependency] += 1 # Place all elements with indegree 0 in queue
 queue = [k for k in nodes.keys() if indegrees[k] == 0] final_order = [] # Continue until all nodes have been dealt with
 while len(queue) > 0: # node of current iteration is the first one from the queue
 curr = queue.pop(0) final_order.append(curr) # remove the current node from other dependencies
 for dependency in nodes[curr]: indegrees[dependency] -= 1 if indegrees[dependency] == 0: queue.append(dependency) # check for circular dependencies
 if len(final_order) != len(nodes): raise Exception("Circular dependency found.") return final_order

Let's try this with a slightly more evolved network:

The corresponding input looks like this:

nodes = { "A": ["B", "C"], "B": ["C", "D"], "C": ["F"], "D": ["F"], "E": ["F"], "F": []
}

If we run this we get the following order:

order = topological_sort(nodes)
print(order) # ['A', 'E', 'B', 'C', 'D', 'F']

Take another look at the graph and convince yourself that that's indeed a correct order that avoids dependency clashes!

Phil Tenteromano - Jun 24

Once suspended, leopfeiffer will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, leopfeiffer will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by leopfeiffer will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If leopfeiffer is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

item.title: CircleCI 拆分配置球！ | CircleCI Split Config Orb!
item.description:
我创建了一个名为 Split Config 的 CircleCI Orb。 这是我私人有趣的编码:)

  • 你想分割你的配置文件吗？

    你有没有想过你是否可以拆分你的 CircleCI 项目的config.yml文件？ 特别是当存储库是一种 MonoRepo 时，它可能会更大，它在一个存储库中包含多个服务。

    好消息！ 拆分配置球在这里为您服务！

    拆分配置球

    您可以将config.yml拆分为多个配置文件。 当 CircleCI 启动并使用合并的配置执行管道时，此 Orb 将拆分配置合并到一个 YAML 中。

    例子

    假设您的存储库中有多个服务，如下所示：

    好吧，它是空的，但请想象每个服务的源代码都在那里:)

    每个服务都有一个用于服务的config.yml 。 例如。 如何使用

    超级简单。 你只需要准备这个config.yml

     version : 2.1 setup : true orbs : split-config : bufferings/split-config@0.1.0 workflows : generate-config : jobs : - split-config/generate-config : find-config-regex : .*/\.circleci/config\.yml

    然后 Orb 根据您指定的正则表达式找到配置文件，并将它们合并到一个 YAML 文件中。 之后，Orb 使用生成的 YAML 文件执行 CircleCI 管道。

    它使用 CircleCI 的动态配置功能来启动一个新的管道。 默认情况下，该功能被禁用，因此请 启用该功能以开始使用。

    然后你会看到这样的东西：

    就这样！

    您对它如何合并配置感兴趣吗？

    它如何合并配置？

    此外，它还具有将 CUE 配置导出为 YAML 的功能。

    我使用这些 CUE 功能来合并您的 YAML 配置。 如果您想了解更多关于 CUE 或它如何合并多个 CUE 配置的信息，请查看 CUE 规范！

    更多细节

    请查看 GitHub 中的 README：
    我写了一些带有工作示例的用例！ 享受更小的配置文件



原文:

I created a CircleCI Orb named Split Config. This is my private fun coding :)

  • https://circleci.com/developer/orbs/orb/bufferings/split-config

Do you want to split your configuration file?

Have you ever thought if you could split the config.yml file of your CircleCI projects? Especially it could be bigger when the repository is a type of MonoRepo which includes multiple services in one repository.

Good news! Split Config Orb is here for you!

Split Config Orb

You can split your config.yml into multiple configuration files. This Orb merges the split configs into one YAML when CircleCI starts and execute a pipeline with the merged configuration.

Example

Let's say you have multiple services in your repo like this:

Well, it's empty but please imagine the source codes of each services are there :)

Each service has a config.yml for the service. For example. the config for service1 is as follows:

version: 2.1 jobs: service1-say-hello: docker: - image: cimg/base:stable steps: - checkout - run: name: "Say hello" command: "echo Hello, World!1" workflows: service1-say-hello-workflow: jobs: - common-say-hello - service1-say-hello

The Orb merges these config files.

How to use

It's super simple. You just need to prepare this config.yml

version: 2.1 setup: true orbs: split-config: bufferings/split-config@0.1.0 workflows: generate-config: jobs: - split-config/generate-config: find-config-regex: .*/\.circleci/config\.yml

Then the Orb finds the config files according to the regex you specified, and merge them into one YAML file. After that, the Orb executes CircleCI pipeline with the generated YAML file.

It uses the Dynamic Config feature of CircleCI to start a new pipeline. By default, the feature is disabled, so please enable the feature to get started.

Then you will see something like this:

That's all!

Are you interested in how it merges the configs?

How does it merge the configs?

The Split Config Orb uses CUE to merge the YAML files.

CUE can import YAML file, convert them into CUE format, and merge them into one CUE config. In addition, it has a feature to export CUE config as YAML.

I used these CUE features to merge your YAML configs. CUE is so powerful but I only use a small part of it. If you want to know more about CUE or how it merges multiple CUE configs, please check CUE specification!

For more details

Please check the README in GitHub:
https://github.com/bufferings/orb-split-config

I wrote some usecases with working examples! Enjoy smaller configuration files
item.author: bufferings

item.title: 在 Typescript 中使用 Partial 构建对象 | Use Partial to Build Objects in Typescript
item.description:
当我们试图对一个对象严格以适应接口并创建它时，打字稿编译器有时会很烦人。

例如，我们有需要所有属性的User界面。

 interface User { username : string ; password : string ; roles : string []; }

但是，在我们的代码中，我们想要创建一个用户并可能调用 API 来获取要分配的角色。 如果我们尝试创建没有所有属性的对象，编译器会显示错误，因为它不适合接口。

在大多数情况下，我们将属性更改为可选roles?: string[]并允许编译，但我们知道这是作弊。

相反，要做到这一点，请使用 Typescripts utils Partial创建具有可用属性的对象，然后添加更多。

 let admin : Partial < User > = { username : ' admin ' , password : ' admin ' }

完美，编译很开心，我们得到了预期的代码:)

了解有关Typescript 实用程序类型的更多信息

照片 de Suzi Kim和Unsplash

一旦暂停，danywalls 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，danywalls 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，danywalls 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 danywalls 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。



原文:

The typescript compiler is sometimes annoying when we try to be strict with one object to fit the interfaces and create it.

For example, we have the interface User with all properties are required.

interface User { username: string; password: string; roles: string[];
}

But, in our code, we want to create a user and maybe call the API to get the roles to assign. If we try to make the object without all the properties, the compiler shows an error because it doesn't fit with the interface.

In most cases, we change the property to optional roles?: string[] and to allow compiling, but we know it is a cheat.

Instead, to do it, use Typescripts utils Partial to create the object with the available properties and later add more.

let admin: Partial<User> = { username: 'admin', password: 'admin'
}

Perfect, the compiles are happy, and we have the code as expected :)

Learn more about Typescript Utility Types

Foto de Suzi Kim en Unsplash

Once suspended, danywalls will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, danywalls will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by danywalls will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If danywalls is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.
item.author: danywalls

item.title: 用更少的时间编写更多的代码 | More Code in Less Time
item.description:
沙里查特胡迪斯

发表于即使你是一个早起的人☀️，你也可能需要早上的一些时间来开始工作。

对我来说，在我真正开始编写新代码之前，最耗时的部分是“重新加载”我正在做的事情的上下文并了解下一步是什么。
我对这个背景和下一步越清楚——对我来说，开始的速度就越快（也就越少吓人）！

结束一天的最佳方式

这就是为什么我总是尝试这样结束我的日子：

  • 关于我在哪里停止的提示，即特定评论，例如//look at <component name> and create a similar version in <other place>或//test also for mobile
  • 如果我可以在提交中结束这一天，那是最好的，但如果我没有足够的东西 - stash + 一个非常易读的名字！

结束一天的最糟糕方式

  • 在一堆 sh*t 💩 中间，有很多代码被注释掉，控制台日志，不知道发生了什么！
  • 在这种情况下，如果我的时间到了（孩子们......），最好恢复到一个干净的状态并在明天重新开始，但我再次给自己留下一个提示或特定任务，例如： //Make this load only when needed 。

你下班后的日常安排是怎样的？ 或者，您还有更多提示可以让流程继续进行吗？ 雅库布·安德杰耶夫斯基—— 一旦暂停，saritchaethudis 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，saritchaethudis 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，saritchaethudis 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 saritchaethudis 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。



原文:

Sarit Chaet Hudis

Posted on Jul 21

Even if you're a morning person ☀️, you probably need some time in the morning to start being productive.

For me, the most time-consuming part until I actually start writing new code is "reloading" the context of what I'm doing and understanding what is the next step.
And the more this context and next step is clear to me- the faster (and less intimidating) it is for me to get going!

The Best Way to End Your Day

That's why I try to always end my days like this:

  • A hint about where I'm stopping, i.e a specific comment like //look at <component name> and create a similar version in <other place> or //test also for mobile
  • If I can end the day in a commit, that's best but if I don't have anything wrapped up enough- stash + a very readable name!

The Worst Way to End Your Day

  • In the middle of a pile of sh*t 💩- with lots of code commented out, console logs and not having any idea what's going on!
  • In this case, if my time's up (kids...) it's better to revert to a clean slate and start anew tomorrow, but again I leave myself a hint or specific task like: //Make this load only when needed.

What's your end-of-day routine like? Or, do you have any more tips to get the flow going? 😊 Leave your thoughts here!

Jakub Andrzejewski - Jul 11

Once suspended, saritchaethudis will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, saritchaethudis will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by saritchaethudis will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If saritchaethudis is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.
